issue_title: A few built-in comparators would be useful
issue_body:
e.g. ANY in `mock` that allows us to skip certain argument comparison, or StrContains/ContainsKeyValue as in `mox` that verify part of the input, etc. Should be straightforward to implement.

I was thinking a recording object would be helpful as well. Say instead of passing in a well defined input to `with_args()` we can simply pass in an object that records what occurs to it and it'll be much easier to partially verify the correctness of the arguments. Something like:
```
class Foo(object):
   def bar(name, age)
     ...
foo = Foo()
expect(foo).bar.with_args(recording).once()
foo.bar('anything', 22)
assert recording.age == 22
```

I can have a pull request if you guys don't have the bandwidth.

issue_title: Continue to allow access to _table_element_finder class
issue_body:
Per discussion with @pekkaklarck on the Slack group...

## Essential Issue

Having created some Python helper functions, using the **Selenium2Library**, I have been able to leverage some of the private methods to accomplish some useful things for my testing framework. An example below shows a function `get_column_number` which used to be able to return the column number of whatever (valid) column _name_ was passed in:

```python
from robot.libraries.BuiltIn import BuiltIn

def _get_sel2lib():
    """== Gets Current Selenium 2 Instance from Robot Framework ==
    
    - Uses the Robot Framework API to get an object of the current Selenium 2 instance.
    - This is an internal helper function only. 
    
    == Calling ==
    
    | *Args* | [none] | |
    | *Returns* | ``object`` | An object instance of the current Selenium 2 library.
    | *Raises* | [none] | |
    """
    return BuiltIn().get_library_instance('Selenium2Library')

def get_column_number(table_locator, col_text, loglevel='INFO'):
    """== Returns Number of Specified Column ==
    
    Returns the number of the first column found which contains the ``col_text`` string.

    - Does not require an exact match; if there is a column named ``Foobar`` and you input ``Foo`` then it will match.
    
    === Calling ===
    
    | *Args* | ``table_locator`` (str) | The table locator containing the column whose number you desire. |
    |        | ``col_text`` (str) | The string of the column you want to locate. |
    | *Returns* | ``int`` | Number of column containing string ``col_name``. |
    | *Raises* | AssertionError | If ``col_name`` cannot be found in the table specified. |

    === Example in Robot ===

    | ${column_number} =    Get Column Number    xpath=//*[@id="deviceListHeader"]    Unsafe
    """
    sel2lib = _get_sel2lib()
    locators = sel2lib._table_element_finder._parse_table_locator(table_locator, 'header')
    found_it = False
    for locator in locators:
        elements = sel2lib._element_finder.find(sel2lib._current_browser(), locator)
        counter = 0
        for element in elements:
            counter += 1
            if col_text in element.text:
                output = counter
                found_it = True
                break
    if found_it is False:
        sel2lib.log_source(loglevel)
        raise AssertionError("No column containing '{}' found in the table identified via {}!".format(col_text, table_locator))
    return output
```

With the _new_ **SeleniumLibrary**, I get an error: `AttributeError: 'SeleniumLibrary' object has no attribute '_table_element_finder'` as that structure doesn't exist as-is. I do notice, however, that [in tableelementfinder.py there is a method named `find_by_header`](https://github.com/robotframework/SeleniumLibrary/blob/master/src/SeleniumLibrary/locators/tableelementfinder.py#L70) which I could leverage _if_ I could access it.

## General Request

Provide a means to access some of these methods to get more granular access to some of the inner workings of the Selenium library.

issue_title: simplejson generates invalid json with iterable_as_array and indent is set
issue_body:
I've found a strange issue with iterable_as_array=True, indent=any_integer_value and empty iterable.

It's easy to reproduce:
```
In [1]: from simplejson import dumps

In [2]: x=(i for i in [])

In [3]: dumps(x, iterable_as_array=True, indent=2)
Out[3]: '\n]'
```
Yes, it's newline and right square bracket.

There is no issue without indent option:
```
In [6]: dumps(x, iterable_as_array=True)
Out[6]: '[]'
```


I'm using latest version of simplejson (3.8.0) with python 3.5.

issue_title: Move some docs to markdown
issue_body:
Read the docs can use markdown with Sphinx: https://blog.readthedocs.com/adding-markdown-support/

I'm not a huge fan of restructured text, so this could be nice. We might also be able to simplify the process of putting the example notebooks into the docs--in an ideal world, we would no longer need pandoc for building the docs.

issue_title: Installer 0.7.2. Page title never changes with the instaled option Twitter Bootstrap Theme.
issue_body:
Hello,

When you install djangocms with the installer using the Bootstrap Theme, the name of the Page title always is displayed "This is my new project home page", because this never change.

The problem is when the djangocms-installer create a new project in some templates this tag is missing:
{% block title %}{% page_attribute 'title' %}{% endblock title %}.

This no happen if you install without the bootstrap option, because the tag exists.

Below templates name that  they are missing the prevous tag: 
page.htlm and feature.htlm.

Please, can you fix it for the next release?

Thank you.
Jeyme

issue_title: recommended method of use in crontabs?
issue_body:
This may just be a documentation issue, but what's the recommended way of using console scripts provided by a package that has been installed into virtualenv managed by pipenv in a crontab?

With traditional virtualenvs, you'd just use the absolute path to `bin/{whatever}`, but with opaque virtualenvs I'm guessing there is or should be a different way.

issue_title: 'pipenv install' fails with 'ssh: Could not resolve hostname my.domain.com:gitlab_username Name or service not known'
issue_body:
##### Issue description

'pipenv install' fails since version 2018.6.25 if the Pipfile contains a package reference to a Gitlab repository, like e.g.: `my_package = {git = "git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git", ref = 
"master"}`

Here, my.domain.com is a replacement for the properly resolving domain.

_Downgrading to 2018.5.18 resolves the issue._


##### Expected result

It should install 'my_package'.

##### Actual result

```
Obtaining my_package from git+ssh://git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git@856f8ff9146dc7fbfc1ae3595203a9f3af8cf023#egg=my_package

issue_title: Misusage of `resolve_deps` function?
issue_body:
There is something wrong with the call to `resolve_deps` here: https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/resolver.py#L56

Please, check the function definition here: https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/utils.py#L394
I am not sure what is the fix though and can't propose a change.

Thank you!

Is this project considers to be an official replacement for pip's requirements.txt?

issue_title: --relocatable seems not do it work right
issue_body: I use the `--relocatable` relocated my env,  and than I moved my env, but after that, my `pip freeze` always git the global list, not the list in the env. I fixed this error by the guide of [this](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/20952797/pip-installing-in-global-site-packages-instead-of-virtualenv) question's accepted answer by modifing the #42 of bin/active, which still is the old path of the env.

issue_title: Extra EOL breaking line numbers
issue_body:
This seems to be the exact opposite of #126.

While working on [linter-js-yaml](https://github.com/AtomLinter/linter-js-yaml) I kept running into a strange bug where the linter was reporting errors on the file on lines past the end of the file. I've traced it down to the fact that it is adding an EOL character to the end of the input, which causes some of the messages to be "past" the input.

For example, if you have this as the contents of the file:
```
key:
  subkey: "Value"
a
```

Then the contents of the buffer passed to this are:
```
JSON.stringify(TextEditor.getText())
""key:\n  subkey: \"Value\"\na""
```

However the message received from this linter is running on the following:
```
JSON.stringify(message.mark.buffer)
""key:\n  subkey: \"Value\"\na\n\u0000""
```

This leads to it reporting `can not read a block mapping entry; a multiline key may not be an implicit key in "test.yml" at line 4, column 1: ^` on a file that only has 3 lines.

For now I will be working around this by capping the line number at the maximum for the file, but this seems like poor behavior on the part of the linter to be reporting things that don't exist.

issue_title: Breaking change in latest release – change version number?
issue_body:
Hi, this isn't really a bug, but more of a versioning thing.

My understanding was that 2.4.1.dev1 should be fully backwards compatible with 2.x.

We just had to change this:
```
kafka_client.cluster.get_offset_manager(consumer_group)
```
to this:
```
kafka_client.cluster.get_group_coordinator(consumer_group)
```
to fix a breaking change in our pykafka upgrade from 2.2.1 to 2.4.1.dev1.

It looks like the change happened from 2.2.1 to 2.3.0.

In 2.2.1: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.2.1/pykafka/cluster.py#L369

In 2.3.0: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2.3.0/pykafka/cluster.py#L369

Should an alias perhaps be created for the old method name (perhaps with a deprecation warning), to maintain backwards compatibility?

issue_title: Support languages other than Python in streamparse.
issue_body:
Publishing a team discussion here.

@amontalenti says:

> [@amontalenti and @rduplain] discussed the whacky idea of making streamparse the core project for “multi-lang Storm”, handling Clojure, Perl, Python, Ruby, Node, whatever else can actually run in Storm, and providing a project management structure and protocol for combining these using Thrift, a specific JAR format, and command-line tooling.

@dan-blanchard says:

> That's kind of crazy, but that's the use case I was dealing with at ETS, so I've got experience making streamparse play nice with a mixed Perl/Python/Java topology.
> 
> Definitely more of a version 4.0 kind of idea though (where 3.0 is the version where we pull out pystorm).
	
@rduplain says:

> Pulling out pystorm might be the time to make streamparse less Python specific, in that all Python bits for spouts/bolts/workflow go into pystorm.
	
@dan-blanchard says:

> I think we should only move the actual multi-lang implementation over to pystorm, otherwise it's not really a split. It's just renaming the current streamparse to pystorm and making streamparse something else entirely.
> 
> I would like pystorm to be just the multi-lang implementation so that pyleus and streamparse can both rely on it, instead of having two projects that have implemented the exact same thing that each need to be updated whenever the protocol changes.
> 
> The Python DSL is somewhat Python-specific (unless we think Ruby devs are going to want to write their topologies in Python), but that should definitely stay in streamparse.
	
@rduplain says:

> If we go all in on the thift-based DSL, then it should be easier to support DSLs in whatever language you want. The whacky idea is then: streamparse design has various hooks to integrate a language. Things like spout/bolt spec, topology definition DSL, jar packing/unpacking hooks/callbacks, submit hooks/callbacks, ...
> 
> I'm suggesting then that pystorm also include modules for these streamparse hooks. You'd then have a collection of tested operations to use in your own storm topology, which are designed for streamparse but could be used in a DIY way.
> 
> Could be 3.0 or 4.0. I think it's worth thinking through the design in the near term to decide whether we want to support integrating other languages in streamparse. Multiple languages in a single topology is very interesting. It sounds like you did this at ETS.
Show less
	
@dan-blanchard says:

> By "thrift-based DSL", do you just mean creating a "sparse serialize" command that will convert language-specific DSLs into a valid StormTopology thrift struct? Would that mean we'd be taking responsibility for maintaining more code in many different languages?
> 
> It seems to me that the best approach here might be to try to contribute a set of utilities for managing multi-lang projects back into Storm proper. We could just expand the "storm jar" command to take a thrift topology definition and a separate jar, add some fairly generic hooks that would run whenever a ShellBolt or ShellSpout started up for environment setup (executing a script contained inside the JAR, for example), and then add that to the official Multi-Lang documentation. That way people who wanted to write their own language-specific DSLs would have some guidance on what they needed to do.
> 
> One alternative to expanding "storm jar" would be to make a "storm thrift" command or something like that that would take a thrift topology definition and a path to some scripts so it could package that all up into a jar first and then submit things as usual.

@rduplain says:

> > Would that mean we'd be taking responsibility for maintaining more code in many different languages?
> 
> No. The idea is to have each language DSL result in a thrift description. Streamparse could call out to DSL implementation by config to get to these thrift files.
> 
> Andrew and I were discussing how much of this should be pushed back into Storm. We'd certainly want to do that, but "incubating" via streamparse or a related project will probably be a productive thing to do if that's our goal.

issue_title: Move test stuff to astropy-helpers if there's a need for it outside astropy-dependents
issue_body:
In APE4 and the current master, the test runner and related framework are *not* in  `astropy-helpers`, but rather in `astropy`.  The justification from @embray in APE4 is that the tests have to be at runtime, so it makes sense to leave it in `astropy` given that affiliated packages will need it at runtime anyway.

But while working on #72 I realized an issue with that: the test runner and test command are now in separate packages, so they can't be kept in sync.  More generally, it's really confusing to figure out how they interplay when they are in different packages.  So maybe we should just move all the testing stuff to `astropy-helpers` too?

cc @astrofrog

issue_title: Use `plane, row, col` dimensions ordering as specified in the `skimage` conventions page
issue_body:
## Description
I've discovered that the declared recommended order of 3D image dimensions is `plane, row, col` (see the table in http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/user_guide/numpy_images.html#coordinate-conventions).
In `skimage` there are couple of places, where this order is violated (at least, in docstrings). One of the cases I can remember is https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/restoration/inpaint.py#L82 . Probably, there are more.

Those mistakes have to be fixed so to not confuse the users.

issue_title: measure.label is documented under morphology.label
issue_body: In the [measure API reference](http://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/api/skimage.measure.html) label is not documented, but it is [documented under morphology module](http://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/api/skimage.morphology.html#label) (which is depreciated).

issue_title: Create documentation for swarming with description.py and permutations.py
issue_body: People want to swarm with more flexibility, and the route of defining a swarm with JSON isn't enough. The documentation for swarming with the `description.py` / `permutations.py` interface is non-existent and needs to be created.

issue_title: Remove GeospatialCoordinateEncoder's altitude capability to enable AnomalyLikelihood capability
issue_body: As described in https://github.com/numenta/nupic/issues/3077, there is a bug in the GeospacialCoordinateEncoder that prevents usage of the AnomalyLikelihood post-process. This proposal is to temporarily remove the `altitude` processing capability of the GeospatialCoordinateEncoder in order to enable AnomalyLikelihood capabilities.

issue_title: TemporalMemory: the maxNewSynapseCount handling has a unsigned int underflow bug
Rename basic_auth_backdoor to basic_auth_activated
issue_body:
Previously, this was used to let anyone connect to any user by only specifying the username and any password.

This has changed and is now acting like basic authentication, letting anyone connect with basic authentication (but each username/password combination is considered unique).

issue_title: Use pyramid subscriber predicates rather than defining our own
issue_body: Pyramid defines what they call [subscriber predicates](http://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/pyramid/en/latest/narr/hooks.html#subscriber-predicates). We should use it rather than defining our own logic (also, it will be cleaner :-))

I have a custom framing protocol handled by hardware below the application, but I'm interested in utilizing C/python generator for structured messages. I may fork libsbp to remove the framing and strip down some of the built-in functionality. 

Any interest in what this would look like? Have you considered something like framing "plug-ins" that would allow users to define their own framing protocol or remove it entirely? I know it's outside the scope of Swift devices, but the work in place for defining message formats and autogenerating structs/classes is very attractive for other embedded projects.

```
import math
from customerio import CustomerIO

cio = CustomerIO(site_id, api_key)
data = {'id': 1, 'email': 'test@test.test', 'value': math.nan}

```

Actual: code runs fine, nothing happens in interface

issue_title: installation: fresh install sql error when mysql is configured with STRICT_ALL_TABLES
issue_body:
Originally by *vbanos* on 2012-02-13

In a fresh invenio installation, when you try to do: 

```
invenio/bin/inveniocfg --create-tables
```

and you have mysql configured with sql-mode = STRICT_ALL_TABLES  (my.cnf configuration value), the command fails with the following message:

```
>> Going to create and fill tables...
Testing DB connection... ok
Testing Python/MySQL/MySQLdb UTF-8 chain... ok
ERROR 1101 (42000) at line 2712: BLOB/TEXT column 'setDescription' can't have a default value
ERROR: failed execution of /opt/invenio/bin/dbexec < /opt/invenio/lib/sql/invenio/tabcreate.sql
```

If you change the sql-mode to the default, then this command is completed successfully.

Strict sql mode is very important because the default mysql configuration is "forgiving" to untidy sql and minor syntactic errors
(check out http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.0/en/server-sql-mode.html#sqlmode_strict_all_tables for more information)

Using strict sql mode, you can avoid many programming errors and build better programs.

Invenio should install correctly when mysql sql-mode=STRICT_ALL_TABLES

issue_title: RFC: new bibcirculation module
issue_body:
# Problem
The bibcirculation module might need an overhaul.

# Proposal
As discussed with the library team, the bibcirculation module should be rewritten from scratch, so the purpose of this RFC is to discuss current development stages.

## Current stage: Data structure
It's about what to store in a database.

Two ideas:
```
    1+-----------------+                                          
+--->|Record           |                                          
|    +-----------------+                                          
|    |Won't change this|                                          
|    +-----------------+                                          
|                                                                 
|            +---------------------------------+                  
|            v1                                |                  
|    +--------------+        n+------------+   |                  
|    |ItemManager   |    +--->|Item        |   |                  
|    +--------------+    |    +------------+   |                  
+----+record        |    |    |item_manager+---+                  
     |items: []     +----+ +--+infos       |                      
     |requesters: []+--+   |  |status: []  |                      
     +--------------+  |   |  |history: [] +---------------+      
                       |   |  +------------+               |      
+----------------------+   |           ^n                  |      
|                          |           |                   vn     
|   n+-----------+1        |           |             +-----------+
+---^+Borrower   +^--------+           |             |Event      |
     +-----------+                     |             +-----------+
     |           |                     |             |date       |
     |loans: []  +---------------------+             |description|
     |history: []+---------------------------------->|borrower   |
     ------------+                                  n|item       |
                                                     +-----------+
```

```
    1+-----------------+                       
+--->|Record           |                       
|    +-----------------+                       
|    |Won't change this|                       
|    +-----------------+                       
|                                              
|           +---------------------------------+
|           vn                                |
|    +--------------+        1+-----------+   |
|    |Item          |    +--->|Borrower   |   |
|    +--------------+    |    +-----------+   |
+----+record        |    |    |loans: []  +---+
     |borrower      +----+    |history: []+---+
     |status: []    |         +-----------+   |
+----+history: []   |               ^n        |
|    |requesters: []+---------------+         |
|    +--------------+                         |
|                                             |
|                                             |
|   n+-----------+n                           |
+--->|Event      |<---------------------------+
     +-----------+                             
     |date       |                             
     |description|                             
     |borrower   |                             
     |item       |                             
     +-----------+                             
```

* I know calling any class '*Manager' is a bad idea, but well ^^
* Both outlines should be able to handle the current use cases of
  * Simple loans
  * Simple loans, with unavailable item(s)
  * inter library loan
  * purchase request
* ItemManager?
  * The ItemManager would just handle the steps required for items which are not immediately available
  * The same functionality could be provided using some more elaborate (maybe not?) database queries
* The Event objects are proposed to keep track of loans and item statuses in order to satisfy the need to generate meaningful statistics
* The attributes are dummies

Personally, I suggest the second approach.

Criticism and ideas are highly encouraged :)

issue_title: OrderedContainer uses the wrong name in `self._order` and rejects ascii keys under Python 3
issue_body:
It uses `z.c.contained.setitem()` to put the data in `self._data`. That function will decode a `bytes` key if needed before storing it with the provided `__setitem__` function. But OrderedContainer stores the original value, not the decoded value. When you then go to delete the item, it removes it from `_order` by the key that you provide. 

This is more-or-less fine on Python 2 because of the implicit conversions between bytes and unicode---assuming the default locale stays the same everywhere:
```pycon
>>> from zope.container.ordered import OrderedContainer
>>> oc = OrderedContainer()
>>> oc[b'abc'] = 1
>>> del oc[u'abc']
```

On Python 3 there is an explicit pre-check that prevents bytes from even getting into `setitem`:
```pycon
>>> from zope.container.ordered import OrderedContainer
>>> oc = OrderedContainer()
>>> oc[b'abc'] = 1
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "//.tox/py36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/zope/container/ordered.py", line 201, in __setitem__
    "ascii or unicode string" % key)
TypeError: 'b'abc'' is invalid, the key must be an ascii or unicode string
```

This pre-check is not actually tested. If we remove the pre-check so that `setitem` can do its decoding thing, then we can't remove the data:
```
>>> from zope.container.ordered import OrderedContainer
>>> oc = OrderedContainer()
>>> oc[b'abc'] = 1
>>> del oc[u'abc']
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "//.tox/py36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/zope/container/ordered.py", line 244, in __delitem__
    self._order.remove(key)
  File "//.tox/py36/lib/python3.6/site-packages/persistent/list.py", line 84, in remove
    self.__super_remove(item)
  File "//.tox/py36/lib/python3.6/collections/__init__.py", line 1084, in remove
    def remove(self, item): self.data.remove(item)
ValueError: list.remove(x): x not in list
```

This is a pretty easy fix

issue_title: Documentation recommends using Python 3.3+ while specific functions used are only available from Python 3.4+
issue_body:
The documentation https://github.com/sensepost/objection/wiki/Installation mentions:
```
python version 3.3+
```

File "./objection/utils/packages.py", line 466, in set_application_binary:
```
    info_plist = plistlib.load(f)
```

From Python documentation (https://docs.python.org/3/library/plistlib.html):
```
plistlib.load(fp, *, fmt=None, use_builtin_types=True, dict_type=dict)
Read a plist file. fp should be a readable and binary file object. Return the unpacked root object (which usually is a dictionary).
[...]
New in version 3.4.
```

As a result objection crashes when using plistlib.load with Python 3.3.

Documentation should be updated to mention Python 3.4+ requirement or code must be changed to be compatible with Python 3.3.

issue_title: Buildozer recompiles p4a when a custom for of plyer is used.
issue_body:
To reproduce :

• Make a fork of plyer
• Have it refered in your buildozer.spec : requirements.source.plyer = path/to/plyer

And everytime you launch the command "buildozer -v android debug", buildozer recompiles p4a.

• Remove the line requirements.source.plyer = path/to/plyer
• Define P4A_plyer_DIR environement variable to your plyer fork.

And it works! (without recompiling p4a and everything...)

issue_title: swift: md5sum changed - fix URL on a static content
issue_body:
Not sure why this happens, but I would suggest to use an URL which never changes (eg. based on a tarball for a specific version). Just to make it more failsave.
However, I'm not the owner of this recipe, so not my decision to do that.

```
File 4123e6579309cd65a5ba3800e0b674f348c62bfb md5 check failed (got 35890521cc6adf9b7ef4bb4689d73f10 instead of 99cf78ea0b4aeb23a2439dd886f00f8f).
Ensure the file is correctly downloaded, and update MD5S_swift
```

issue_title: Pandas 0.22.0 `DeprecationWarning` from `util.py:652`
issue_body:
The warning basically says it all:
```
/Users/<user>/anaconda2/envs/py3k/lib/python3.6/site-packages/patsy/util.py:652: DeprecationWarning: pandas.core.common.is_categorical_dtype is deprecated. import from the public API: pandas.api.types.is_categorical_dtype instead
  return safe_is_pandas_categorical_dtype(data.dtype)
```
Would be good to switch over to the new API soon to get rid of the warning and prevent breakage with future `pandas` versions.

issue_title: Confusing behavior on typos when keyword args are reinterpreted as positional arguments
issue_body:
Let's say I have some function like this:

```
def myfunction(arg1, arg2, run_encoding):
     print(arg1, arg2, run_encoding)

fire.Fire()
```

If I accidentally misspell the run_encoding argument, I get behavior that can be really confusing to debug (e.g., if that's a relative file path, you suddenly get a `--mypath=1` directory):

```
›› python fire_example.py myfunction --arg1=a --arg2=b --run-encoding=5
('a', 'b', 5)
›› python fire_example.py myfunction --arg1=a --arg2=b --runencoding=5                                                                                                           
('a', 'b', '--runencoding=5')
```

I assume this happens because fire fills in all the keyword args and then assumes you specify positional arguments after them?

What I wish happened instead is that, unless explicitly quoted, any argument starting with `--` would be treated as a keyword arg, so you'd see:

```
1. Initial component
2. Accessed property "myfunction"
3. ('The function received no value for the required argument:', 'run_encoding')

Type:        function
String form: <function myfunction at 0x110138cf8>
File:        /Users/jtratner/fire_example.py
Line:        3

Usage:       fire_example.py myfunction ARG1 ARG2 RUN_ENCODING
             fire_example.py myfunction --arg1 ARG1 --arg2 ARG2 --run-encoding RUN_ENCODING
```

This is related to the issue in https://github.com/google/python-fire/issues/25, in that if positional arguments were not allowed after keyword arguments, you wouldn't end up with the ambiguity, but I can see how that's less "shell like".

issue_title: Unit conversion error
issue_body:
units.kilometers does not handle the feet argument properly, the issue is specifically with line 64:

> miles += feet / ft(1.)

followed by line 67:

> ret += miles * 1.609344

feet/ft(1.) is of course the number of kilometers, not miles.  So running the following test:

> Distance(feet=1).feet

1.609344

This can be fixed by changing line 64 to:

> miles += feet / ft(miles=1.)

issue_title: Add pandas doc section, address the rate-limiting issue
issue_body:
Geocoding a pandas dataframe with a large amount of rows seems like a common request. The issue people usually face is that the services (namely Nominatim, which is considered as the "default" geocoder in the docs and code examples) start rate-limiting the requests which results in an exception being thrown.

The issue should be covered by the docs (as a separate section), explaining why the rate-limiting is applied (b/c online services have limited capacity) and how to overcome it (geocode with delays/use a different service/a selfhosted service/a paid plan).

I feel that without covering this subject in the docs we might eventually get the default geopy user_agent banned permanently on Nominatim side (see #314), which is extremely undesirable.

Perhaps along with the docs a utility class handling the rate limiting exceptions might be added (something similar to #99 / http://www.jackmaney.com/2015/01/09/geocoding-rate-limited-queue/).

Also there's a `geopandas` package, which contains a wrapper around geopy, which might be useful for pandas users: https://geopandas.readthedocs.io/en/v0.4.0/geocoding.html

Refs:
- #112 (Nominatim user-agent)
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/51393049/convert-county-state-names-to-coordinates
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38031705/http-error-429-too-many-requests-by-python-geopy
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/51127617/geocoderserviceerror-geopy-nominatim-in-local-server
- https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49640197/error-429-too-many-requests-while-geocoding-with-geopy-in-python

issue_title: Breadcrumbs: Python logging message is not formatted properly
issue_body:
Hi guys,
I wrote as simple project spawning 2 logging events (it's a bit rusty, but it was just created for testing reasons):
```
import logging

from aiohttp import ServerDisconnectedError
from raven import setup_logging, Client
from raven.handlers.logging import SentryHandler

DSN = 'https://74f9dc1224494c979e161e8539b66c0b:3e9d2b03a40d4428916d72ddecb004f1@sentry-prod#############'
client = Client(dsn=DSN)
handler = SentryHandler(client, level=logging.WARNING)
setup_logging(handler)


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
logger.setLevel(logging.WARNING)

def retry():
    context = {
        'attempt': 1,
        'attempts': 3,
        'fn': dict.fromkeys,
    }
    exc = ServerDisconnectedError()
    logger.warning(
        exc.__class__.__name__ + ' -> Attempts (%(attempt)d) are over for %(fn)r',
        context,
        exc_info=exc,
    )

    raise exc


def get(url):
    try:
        retry()
    except Exception as exc:
        msg = 'Failed to download %(url)s'
        context = {'url': url}
        logger.exception(msg, context, exc_info=exc)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    get('https://twitter.com/BlankPageApp')
```

This an output which we receive on web UI:

[screen_2](https://www.dropbox.com/s/9glk02mxup1ytgw/screenshot_1.png?dl=0)

Why python log message with `WARNING` logging level is not rendered properly showing log formatting `kwargs` instead of real values?

Thanks,
Alex

issue_title: Connect Sparklingwater to DCOS Mesos Spark cluster ?
make GalSim citable via DOI
Persistence
issue_body:
Some IR detectors have significant persistence where some (small) fraction of the previous image is not fully cleared before taking the next image.  I believe this can already be implemented in GalSim by keeping track of the order of the images, and multiplying by some small values to get an initial image on which to add the values for the next image.  

But it would probably be worth making this effect a bit more native to GalSim, and in particular add it to the config layer, so people can just specify `persistence_factor : 0.01` or whatever to get this effect.

N.B. This is somewhat related to #542, since that implementation also involves having an initial image onto which the new objects should be added.

issue_title: Section iterator strategies
issue_body:
Looks like right now there is no easy way to map section IDs between neuroM and neuron, this makes integration of these packages in some cases too fragile or nearly impossible.
Not sure about other use cases, but in pair recording app neuroM is used to load morphologies, and neuron - to run simulations on small subset of cells from a given circuit.

As I know neuroM sets IDs according to order of sections in morphology file, but neuron - sets them according also to neurite type. In case order of sections in morphology file is not the same to how neuron loads them (according to Arseniy, happens in morphologies coming from external sources, like  AIBS) - mapping sections by their IDs becomes impossible.

My proposal is to add section iterator with optional argument which will define strategy of iteration - like `depth_first`, `neuron`, etc. or something similar.

Could be I'm missing something. Does this looks like something reasonable?

issue_title: re-enable bifurcating soma in special case
issue_body:
It seems issue #475 was over-restrictive, making the commonly used SWC "3 point soma" structure illegal. In this 3-point structure, the second and third soma points are children of the first. This means the first point is a bifurcating soma point. Allow this when

* The soma contains exactly three points
* The first (soma) point is the parent of the second and third soma points

issue_title: [v2] Remove team and organization associations
issue_body:
Commands of these types:

```
tower-cli organization associate_admin
tower-cli team associate
```

Can be replaced with commands of the type:

```
tower-cli role grant --type=member --target-team="teamname" --user="username"
tower-cli role grant --type=admin --organization="orgname" --user="username"
```

Should we consider removing the former from 3.2? Or would it be better to leave those in?

Maybe leave it, I just want to open up the possibility here.

issue_title: Upgrading postgres
issue_body:
I have a patroni running with a single postgres 9.5.4. I want to upgrade postgres to the latest 10.1. But I am not able to find any instructions regarding this. My yml file is attached. Renamed it to .txt for upload to succeed

```
sudo add-apt-repository "deb http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt/ trusty-pgdg main"

3. Stopped both the postgres server

issue_title: Document synchronous_mode_strict in patroni/docs/replication_modes.rst and patroni/docs/SETTINGS.rst
issue_body:
A text would be like the one used in releases.rst where this funcionality was implemented (below). 


### Synchronous replication mode strict

Normally, when synchronous_mode is enabled and there are no replicas attached to the master, Patroni will disable synchronous replication in order to keep the master available for writes. The _synchronous_mode_strict_ option changes that, when it is set Patroni will not disable the synchronous replication in a lack of replicas, effectively blocking all clients writing data to the master. In addition to the synchronous mode guarantee of preventing any data loss due to automatic failover, the strict mode ensures that each write is either durably stored on two nodes or not happening altogether if there is only one node in the cluster.

issue_title: Patroni does not perform failover if master lost data directory
issue_body:
We are migrating from 1.2 to 1.3.4 and one of our tests fails - we removed data directory and are expecting failover to replica and master reinitialization. But with new version master detects empty dir, releases key and .. wait for leader to bootstrap. Meanwhile replica is considering master as "master" and wait for master too. 
From master: 

> 2017-10-11 11:35:27,431 ERROR: get_postgresql_status
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "/patroni/patroni/api.py", line 405, in get_postgresql_status
>     retry=retry)[0]
>   File "/patroni/patroni/api.py", line 381, in query
>     return retry(self.server.query, sql, *params)
>   File "/patroni/patroni/utils.py", line 259, in __call__
>     return func(*args, **kwargs)
>   File "/patroni/patroni/api.py", line 452, in query
>     raise e
> OperationalError: could not open file "base/12404/2600": No such file or directory
> 
> 2017-10-11 11:35:27,433 INFO: released leader key voluntarily as data dir empty and currently leader
> ...
> 2017-10-11 11:35:37,416 INFO: Lock owner: None; I am node1
> 2017-10-11 11:35:37,420 INFO: waiting for leader to bootstrap

From slave:

> 2017-10-11 14:20:50,223 INFO: Got response from node1 http://10.129.1.63:8008/patroni: {"state": "unknown", "database_system_identifier": "6475561736777527351", "role": "master", "patroni": {"scope": "common", "version": "1.3.4"}}
> 2017-10-11 14:20:50,262 WARNING: Master (node1) is still alive
> 2017-10-11 14:20:50,267 INFO: following a different leader because i am not the healthiest node

I suppose that replica cannot promote itself because broken master declares wrong status. Broken master declares wrong status because it released key but never update `postgresql.role` value.

issue_title: Pip install issue in virtual environment? (python 2.7)
issue_body:
### Description
Has anything changed relatively recently with the pip install process? I'm trying to install within a virtual environment and without requiring sudo.

### Expected bahaviour
Package installs without without error.

### Actual behaviour
Install dies with error:

distutils.errors.DistutilsError: Setup script exited with error: could not create '/usr/local/share/man/man1': Permission denied

### Steps to reproduce
virtualenv --no-site-packages env2
. env2/bin/activate
pip install connexion

### Additional info:
So the install seems to be looking outside of virtual environment for this file:
/usr/local/share/man/man1/flake8.1

If it doesn't find this. then it doesn't have the permissions to create it, and bombs out. My workaround just then was to do a global install first, outside of the virtual env, with:

sudo pip install connexion

and then go back into my virtual env and install again - this time the file is found and the install completes.

This issue seems like something introduced in the last few weeks. Is it just my systems or can others verify this? (I've had the same issue on my local dev machine - Ubuntu 16.04 and my Jenkins server Ubuntu 14.04)

thanks in advance,

Dan

issue_title: Expose value-level metadata for parameters in the Web API
issue_body:
Connected to #677 (subset) 

The YAML format used for parameters allows to define metadata *at the value level*. For instance:

```YAML
description: Amount of the basic income
values:
  2015-12-01:
    value: 600
    metadata:
      reference: https://law.gov.example/basic-income/amount/2015-12
  2014-01-01:
    value: 595
    metadata:
      reference: https://law.gov.example/basic-income/amount/2014-01
```

However, the parameter format exposed by the Web API is "too compact" to expose these metadata:

```JSON

{
  "description": "Amount of the basic income", 
  "id": "benefits.basic_income", 
  "values": {
    "2014-01-01": 595.0,
    "2015-12-01": 600.0
  }
}
```

-----

As a client of the API building a front displaying the parameters,

So that I can format a parameter accordingly

issue_title: assiette_cotisations_sociales_prive ne gère pas le temps partiel
issue_body:
J'ai peur qu'il y ait un problème dans une variable appelée pour calculer presque toutes les cotisations sociales : [assiette_cotisations_sociales_prive](http://legislation.openfisca.fr/variables/assiette_cotisations_sociales_prive)
L'assiette est à 1457€ mensuels pour les bas revenus (< 500€/mois), ce qui conduit à des cotisations sociales forfaitaires de 206€ mensuels pour ces bas revenus, d'où un salaire_imposable négatif pour les revenus < 200€/mois.
Or, la formule de cette variable semble douteuse : 
`max_(assiette, smic_proratise * not_(apprenti)) * (assiette > 0)`
En gros, pour les bas revenus, le smic_proratise est appliqué comme assiette, d'où le montant forfaitaire. La condition "assiette > 0" est peut-être à revoir : ne serait-ce pas "assiette > xxx €" ?
Je laisse cette énigme à qui voudra bien se plonger dans les textes de loi.

issue_title: argschema not handling non-required Nested schemas with required fields well
issue_body:
```
import argschema
import marshmallow as mm


class MyNested(argschema.schemas.DefaultSchema):
    a = argschema.fields.Int(required=True)
    b = argschema.fields.Str(required=True)
    c = argschema.fields.Str(required=False,default='c')

class MySchema(argschema.ArgSchema):
    nested = argschema.fields.Nested(MyNested, only=['a', 'b'],
        required=False, default=mm.missing)

def test_nested_example():
    mod = argschema.ArgSchemaParser(schema_type=MySchema,
                                     args = [])
    assert(not 'nested' in mod.args.keys)

def test_nested_marshmallow_example():
    schema = MySchema()
    (result,errors)=schema.load({})
    assert(len(errors)==0)
```

test_nested_example does not pass, but test_nested_marshmallow_example does.  If the user wants to specify a Nested schema which is optional, but if filled in has some required fields, we aren't handling that well now.

issue_title: Chalice local mode drops query string params with blank vlaues
issue_body:
When running Chalice in local mode it, incorrectly parses query string parameters dropping parameters that have no value. This is inconsistent with how Lambda/API gateway performs.  For example, given the following URL http://example.com?a=1&b=&c=3 the resulting query string param collection will not include b in local mode, but would include it when running in Lambda. The correct implementation of parse_qs should include the keep_blank_values=True flag as described [here](https://docs.python.org/2/library/urlparse.html#urlparse.parse_qs)

issue_title: conv2d_bias performance issues
issue_body:
Hi, I replaced the LSTM in a classifier with the following class:
```
class Conv():
    def __init__(self, model, n_gram, filter_size, emb_size):
        self.emb_size = emb_size
        self.filter = model.add_parameters((1, n_gram, emb_size, filter_size))
        self.bias = model.add_parameters((filter_size))
        
    def __call__(self, embeds):
        conv_filter = dy.parameter(self.filter)
        bias = dy.parameter(self.bias)
        seq_len = len(embeds)
        
        embeds = dy.transpose(dy.concatenate_cols(embeds))
        embeds = dy.reshape(embeds, (1, seq_len, self.emb_size))
        conved =  dy.rectify(dy.conv2d_bias(embeds, conv_filter, bias, [1, 1], is_valid=False))
        return conved[0]
```

The LSTM based network ~6 hours while the CONV run for ~7 hours,
As I understand  CONV should be faster.

Is it known? 

Thanks, Tal 👍🏻

*The accuracy of the classifier also suffered but this was sort of expected.

issue_title: Python manual installation instructions fail on Linux (not Mac)
issue_body:
Following the Python manual installation instructions (http://dynet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/python.html#manual-installation) works fine on Mac, but on Linux it only works if you remove one of the `../` values, changing this (which works on Mac)

    cmake .. -DEIGEN3_INCLUDE_DIR=../../eigen -DPYTHON=`which python`

to this (which works on Linux)

    cmake .. -DEIGEN3_INCLUDE_DIR=../eigen -DPYTHON=`which python`

I suspect that this is because we're typing `cmake ..`, which already includes a `..`, but it's bizarre that the results are different in the two environments. It'd be nice if we could resolve this, but if not we might want to go back to recommending using the absolute path.

         b_r = dy.parameter(self.dec_b_r)

         w_ch = dy.parameter(self.dec_w_ch)
         w_hh = dy.parameter(self.dec_w_hh)
         b_h = dy.parameter(self.dec_b_h)

         # dy.colwise_add(w_cz * ctx_watt, b_z)
         logger.debug('w_cz: {} {}'.format(type(w_cz), w_cz.npvalue().shape))
         logger.debug('attention {} {}'.format(type(ctx_watt), ctx_watt.npvalue().shape))
         print (w_cz * ctx_watt).npvalue().shape
         z = dy.logistic(w_cz * ctx_watt + w_hz * s + b_z)    # sigmoid
         r = dy.logistic(w_cr * ctx_watt + w_hr * s + b_r)    # sigmoid
         c_h = w_ch * ctx_watt

         hidden = dy.tanh(dy.dot_product((w_hh * s + b_h), r) + c_h)
         hidden = dy.dot_product(s, z) + dy.dot_product((1. - z), hidden)

         bxm = dy.transpose(bxm)
         if bxm is not None:
             hidden = bxm[:, None] * hidden + (1. - x_m)[:, None] * s.npvalue()
         hshape = hidden.shape     # (src_len, batch)
         hidden_dy = dy.matInput(hshape[0], hshape[1])
         hidden_dy.set(hidden.flatten())
         return hidden_dy

issue_title: Add optional support for WinKerberos
issue_body: [WinKerberos](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/winkerberos) is a kerberos/pykerberos/kerberos-sspi work-alike for Windows SSPI with no dependencies (no need to install pywin32). It is actively maintained, responsive to bug reports, enhancement requests, and pull requests (though I haven't seen any PRs yet). It supports some features kerberos-sspi does not (the ability to authenticate as a different kerberos user than the process owner) and some features [kerberos-sspi can not](https://github.com/may-day/kerberos-sspi/issues/1). it should be a drop in alternative.

issue_title: Documentation Problem in Real World Example
issue_body:
Hey,

in the [real world example](https://github.com/requests/requests-oauthlib/blob/master/docs/examples/real_world_example.rst) you are using Githubs API as a case study and Flask as the framework. It would probably be worth a mention that with Github's API, the `redirect_uri` parameter is optional, especially if you are using flask on the other side, as `flask-oauthlib` will require this parameter and if users want to create something based on that example, this will probably be a minor pitfall.

I can issue a PR to fix this if you agree that this is somewhat confusing.

Cheers

Is anyone seeing this issue in production, or is it just me:

Forbidden: 403 Request had insufficient authentication scopes.

issue_title: libsass miscompiles breadcrumb content
issue_body:
Under the environment:
```
sassc: 3.2.1
libsass: 3.2.4
sass2scss: 1.0.3
```
the SCSS in question:
```scss
content: "#{$breadcrumb-separator}\00a0";
```
is getting compiled to:
```css
content: "/\\00a0";
```
which is incorrect.
By comparison, Ruby Sass compiles this code to:
```css
content: "/ ";
```
which is correct (albeit not ASCII).

This is due to a bug in libsass: https://github.com/sass/libsass/issues/1115

The questions are (a) should bootstrap-sass try to workaround this? (b) if yes, how? (c) if yes, since this only affects libsass, do we make the change in bootstrap-sass, or in upstream Bootstrap?
CC: @twbs/sass

issue_title: respond.js + nested media queries (IE8)
issue_body:
Hi,

using 'img-retina' mixin, it will create a nested media query for retina devices only.

However, in IE8, respond.js would stop parsing the css file as soon as it meets that nested query, leading to some major issues such as potentially entire page not looking great on desktop (if say, you used retina mixin somewhere in the top of your code).

I had to manually move all my retina css to the bottom of my SCSS file so they will be parsed last and respond.js won't mess things up. 

Any advice on how to tackle nested media queries?

I get that the `stack.yaml`  has variable definitions - and those map into the stacker classes `VARIABLES` dictionary.  
I also get that environment variables or cli overrides can replace template variables, similar to templating behaviour. 

Question: 

What is the common practice with parameters that are environment specific,  and as a template/stack author you want to declare as "must override".   The intent being that a "stack consumer" can't deploy your stack without specifying a parameter? 

In a lot of cases the author and consumer are the same person but I'm just using it to help with perspective on this question.

One example is  `VpcId`.   I probably want this to be environment specific, and don't want a default.

I could come up with a scheme like: 

stack.yaml
```
~ 
stacks:
   -name: Test
     variables:
         VpcId: 'must-override'
```

template.py
```
~
 VARIABLES = {
        'VpcId': {'type': EC2VPCId, 'description': 'Vpc Id'},
```

Your output isn't very friendly.  It'll say something like `'must-override' is not a valid VpcId`.

What do people do in this case? 

The reason I ask is if you put a variable in a template and someone comes by to run it - not realizing they should define their own environment file with their own VPC..  (maybe a bad usage example)..  but it might be hard to do. 

Other solutions might be 1) have something generate your stack.yaml (heavier lift).

I think I'm missing something in the documentation or examples.   If so - what am I missing?!

issue_title: PlacementGroup Parameter
Determine, formalize and document guidelines and policy for deprecations.
issue_body:
We need to nail down a formalized policy for deprecations (options, modules, functions, etc) going into and after 1.0.0 that developers can follow that will improve the user experience around deprecations.

This should be documented in the developers guide as well as in the release process docs.

issue_title: Lack of complete manylinux1 wheel support in pex forces pants workarounds for plugins
issue_body:
Pex doesn't yet fully support `manylinux1` wheels; yet, pants is forced to release one to satisfy pypi hosting constraints. When the pants plugin resolver runs, it uses pex resolution machinery and chokes trying to resolve `pantsbuild.pants` as a transitive dependency of the plugin. This is currently worked around by deploying a `pantsbuild.pants` sdist to pypi along-side the platform-specific wheels. The sdist is bogus since it contains a `native_engine.so` for only one platform and the workaround is only effective for pip installs and then only because the pip installed `pantsbuild.pants` wheel is earlier in the `PYTHONPATH` than the `pantsbuild.pants` sdist resolved as plugins' transitive dependency.

Remove deployment of sdists alongside wheels from `build-support/bin/release.sh` when we upgrade to pex with full `manylinux1` support.

See:
+ https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/281
+ https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/pull/316

issue_title: extension objects not visible to test runner
issue_body:
# symptom
We have a plugin to compile cython files into an .so that exposes some extension objects. We'd like to use those extension objects in other python build targets, and also use them in unit tests.
Exposing the so for other targets works, in that the .so appears in the pex. However unit tests don't work. The test runner can't find the .so.

Perhaps there something we are not doing in the plugin code to expose the .so to py.test? Do we need to add something to the pythonpath?

# environment
pants: 1.8.0rc0
ubuntu 17.04
target python for the build artifacts: 3.6.3


# Repro
In [pantsbug.zip](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/files/2138427/pantsbug.zip) is a small toy project that demonstrates the issue. To repro:

```bash
mkdir repro
cd repro
cp ~/Downloads/pantsbug.zip .
unzip pantsbug.zip

# clean slate
rm -rf dist lib/*.h lib/*.so lib/*.cpp /tmp/pantscache && ./pants clean-all

# create pex for app that depends on the cython lib
./pants binary apps/gardener:

# run the pex, this works
dist/gardener.pex

# same thing for the lib itself, also works
dist/shrubbery-lib.pex


# verify the .so are in the pex (they have to be, otherwise running the pex above would not work
unzip -l dist/shrubbery-lib.pex | grep shrubberylib
unzip -l dist/gardener.pex | grep shrubberylib


# running tests does not work sadly, because test runner does not find the .so
./pants test libraries/shrubbery-lib:

# same fail here
./pants test apps/gardener:

```

issue_title: Investigate CI Failure: "ImportError: No module named ..."
issue_body:
This first happened when I was testing v2 engine in master. Then I hit this again when I was working on https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/4239/. I made BuildFileAddressMapper.scan_specs return a set instead of an OrderedSet, since scan_specs does not have strict ordering requirements in pants repo. This change ended up breaking CI with error messages like "ImportError: No module named java.test_nailgun_integration". To pass CI, I kept OrderedSet bit, and opened this ticket to track the issue.

To reproduce, simply change this line: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/build_graph/build_file_address_mapper.py#L184 to be ```addresses = set()```, and then start a Pull Request in travis. I couldn't reproduce it locally.

My current guess is that the failure is related to the order change of "./pants fileter".

"Switching from OrderedSet to set" will change the output order of "./pants filter" task. "filter" is used in ci.sh to determine which integration tests run on which shards. With a different output from  "filter" task, integration tests may be distributed to a different shard than before, thus triggering some caching issues. But still, all these are just theories.

issue_title: Remove `PrepareResources` data dependency on 'classes_by_target'
issue_body: Today the `PrepareResources` task has a [fake data dependency](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/3a71d12e60140cf8c61a102a046fe69e1fe428be/src/python/pants/backend/core/tasks/prepare_resources.py#L36) on jvm compilation to work around an issue Square encounters with annotation processors and global compile classpaths.  Eliminate the fake data dependency when Square is using the isolated jvm compile strategy or else when pants as a whole is.

issue_title: Python resource_targets args not accounted for in `changed`
issue_body:
This is a bug in both v1 and v2.

As of recently, v2 attempts to work around this issue here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/55716def4cf4fb09bb0fdfd3d20f447a5c92e123/src/python/pants/engine/legacy/source_mapper.py#L78-L91 , but unfortunately, it inspects the wrong kwarg. `resource_targets` is the appropriate kwarg, but because v1 also does not handle them, all integration tests pass.

Resolving this should likely wait until we've decided what to do with #3560.

issue_title: The Subsystem class is `:API: public` but is not in fact implementable.
issue_body:
This is due to key portions of its API being abstract, but not also marked `:API: public`.
In particular, its `Optionable` base-class has an abstract `options_scope` field that is not marked `:API: public`, but yet _must_ be filled in by implementors.

This is a much wider problem, but it was specifically raised in #3543.
This is in the same vein as the problems with `Distribution` as noted in #3263.

The proper fix is to fix the [`:API: public` spec](http://pantsbuild.github.io/deprecation_policy.html) to say reasonable things like ~"abstract members of `:API: public` types are implicitly `:API: public` or, better, fixing the spec to treat all "public" members of `:API: public` types as public (recursively), but this requires changing "public" member that should not be to "_private" members and doing other refactors that this causes.

I'm interested to hear what @digwanderlust thinks is a reasonable path forward towards valid `:API: public` annotations in-general.

issue_title: namespace/subsystem are not taken into consideration in multiproc mode
issue_body:
The `namespace` and `subsystem` kwargs add prefixes to metric name when running normally. However, when using the multiprocess mode of the library, those prefixes are ignored.

Here is a small program to reproduce this problem:
```
from prometheus_client import Counter, CollectorRegistry, start_http_server, generate_latest, multiprocess
import time

registry = CollectorRegistry()
c = Counter('test', 'Test', namespace='foo', subsystem='bar', registry=registry)
c.inc()

multiprocess.MultiProcessCollector(registry)
print(generate_latest(registry).decode())
```

Running this program prints:
```
# HELP foo_bar_test Test
# TYPE foo_bar_test counter
foo_bar_test 1.0
# HELP test Multiprocess metric
# TYPE test counter
test 1.0
```

issue_title: Cannot access Heading 1 style
issue_body:
Accessing heading styles by name, as in `document.styles['Heading 1']` generates an error "KeyError: u"no style with name 'Heading 1'"

But accessing styles by id, as in `document.styles['Heading1']` generates a deprecation warning.

Trying to figure out why, I found that within class Styles, `self._element.get_by_name('Heading 1')` works OK and returns the proper style.   But accessing through `document.styles['Heading 1']` goes through `Styles.__getitem__(self, key)`, which processes the key through `BabelFish.ui2internal(key)`, which turns 'Heading 1' into 'heading 1'.   But in my document, the name of the style is 'Heading 1', not 'heading 1'.

Does this mean my document is incorrect ?   It was created from scratch using LibreOffice.

Should I use `document.styles._element.get_by_name('Heading 1')` in order to avoid the deprecation warning ?

issue_title: README Examples Don't Work
issue_body:
Two README examples fail. One:
```python
from plaid import Client
from plaid import errors as plaid_errors
from plaid.utils import json


client = Client()
institutions = json.loads(client.institutions().content)
categories = json.loads(client.categories().content)
```
And it breaks cause the Client doesn't actually allow no params for `client_id` and `secret`.

I also tried this example (with my actual client, username, password, etc):

```python
from plaid import Client
from plaid import errors as plaid_errors
from plaid.utils import json


client = Client(client_id='***', secret='***')
account_type = 'bofa'

try:
    response = client.connect(account_type, {
     'username': '***',
     'password': '***'
    })
except plaid_errors.PlaidError:
     pass
else:
    connect_data = json.loads(response.content)
``` 

This works with a 200 but `response.content` is a byte object and not a string. So json.loads fails. This happens using both python's json library and plaid's json util. `response.text` and `response.json()` still give me what I want so there's a workaround.

I'm using python 3 and it looks like you guys added support for that in the 1.10.

issue_title: Updated event won't tag attributes within objects
issue_body:
Hi, 
it seems there is a bug in pymisp(?) if you want to update an existing event.

If you fetch an event and update it with a new object with tagged attributes, the updated event will only contain the attributes within the object however without any tags.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Get event e.g. via `res= api.search(uuid=<event_uuid>)` 
2. Load event e.g. via `mispevent = MispEvent().load(res.get('response')[0])`
3. Add new object with tagged attributes
```            
mispObject = MISPObject('file')
mispObject.add_attribute('filename', type='filename',
                                         value='filename.exe',
                                         Tag=[{'name':'tlp:amber'}]) 
mispevent.add_object(mispObject)
```
4. Update Event
`api.update(mispevent)`

issue_title: ServiceWorker attempts to process HTML memento if installed upon access
issue_body:
Logic exists in the replay system (where?) to check if the ServiceWorker has been installed upon accessing a memento. This would occur if the replay system is started up for the first time and a URI-M is immediately accessed instead of the root page of the replay system.

ipwb current checks if it's installed and if not, redirects to install the SW then back to the originally accessed URI-M. Upon returning to the URI-M, the SW is processing the HTML page (root memento) and attempting to unsuccessfully reroute.

The console looks like this on first access using the above pattern:
![screen shot 2017-06-23 at 3 29 36 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2514780/27497477-dbd1f502-5828-11e7-94f4-25b51ed31c3a.png)

and like this on subsequent accesses:
![screen shot 2017-06-23 at 3 30 24 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2514780/27497490-ecc2b216-5828-11e7-9bcc-51b52875310f.png)

The errors _only occur_ if the SW is installed upon first access, not if the user navigates from a link on the main replay page.

The issue is likely in checking for `isNavigation = event.request.mode === 'navigate'`. Normally, going from the main replay page to this page would be a navigational event but with the redirection to install the SW then redirecting back, `event.request.mode` is `no-cors`.

This is a minor issue. Replay still works as expected (the attempt to recursively redirect the replay system URI at http://127.0.0.1:5000/memento/20170301192639/http://127.0.0.1:5000/20170301192639/cs.odu.edu/~mkelly/semester/2017_spring/remotefroggie.html fails) but this issue might also come up if a user first installs then accesses a URI, e.g., through following the README instructions.

issue_title: Make dark.reads.Reads.filter return a new Reads instance
issue_body:
It would be slightly more convenient, I think, if `dark.reads.Reads.filter` returned a new `Reads` instance instead of a generator that yields `Read` instances. Then you could do this:
```python
reads = FastaReads(filename).filter(...)
```
and you'd have a `Reads` instance. Right now (once #349 is merged) you'd need to
```python
reads = Reads(FastaReads(filename).filter(...))
```
I think that, given the user is already using a `Reads` instance, we know that they want to work with that class, so returning one from `filter` makes sense.

issue_title: Update a LegalUser with no LegalRepresentativeAddress
issue_body:
For a legal user `LegalRepresentativeAddress` is optional, so you can create one without it. But when retrieving such an user, `LegalRepresentativeAddress` is not `None`, it got something like `{"Region": null, "Country": null, "AddressLine2": null, "AddressLine1": null, "PostalCode": null, "City": null}`. That's OK, but the thing is when you try to save your user (for example after you update some fields, it will send a request where `LegalRepresentativeAddress` is not `null` but either with actual data, just a dictionary with empty values so the API return an error.

## How to reproduce the issue

    from mangopay.resources import User, LegalUser

    my_user = LegalUser(
        name='My Company',
        email='email@gmail.com',
        person_type='LEGAL',
        legal_person_type='BUSINESS',
        headquarters_address=Address(
            address_line_1='10 rue des champs',
            city='Nantes',
            postal_code='44000',
            country='FR',
        legal_representative_first_name='John',
        legal_representative_last_name='Doe',
        legal_representative_birthday=606441600,
        legal_representative_nationality='FR',
        legal_representative_country_of_residence='FR',
    )
    my_user.save()
    
    pk = my_user.get_pk()

    my_user2 = User.get(pk)
    my_user2.save()     # <--- crash here, because API returns an error

## The request sent by the SDK when doing the 2nd save()

**HTTP Method:** PUT
**URL:** https://api.sandbox.mangopay.com/v2.01/<ID_CLIENT>/users/legal/<ID_USER>
**HEADERS:**

* Authorization: bearer <TOKEN>
* User-Agent: MangoPay V2 Python/None
* Content-Type: application/json

**DATA:** `{"LegalRepresentativeCountryOfResidence": "FR", "LegalPersonType": "BUSINESS", "LegalRepresentativeFirstName": "John", "LegalRepresentativeLastName": "Doe", "LegalRepresentativeBirthday": 606441600, "LegalRepresentativeAddress": {"Region": null, "Country": null, "AddressLine2": null, "AddressLine1": null, "PostalCode": null, "City": null}, "HeadquartersAddress": {"Region": null, "Country": "FR", "AddressLine2": null, "AddressLine1": "10 rue des champs", "PostalCode": "44000", "City": "Nantes"}, "Name": "My Company", "LegalRepresentativeNationality": "FR", "Email": "bb@bb.bb", "KYCLevel": "LIGHT", "PersonType": "LEGAL"}`

## API response

`{"Message":"One or several required parameters are missing or incorrect. An incorrect resource ID also raises this kind of error.","Type":"param_error","Id":"2790e064-da7d-44e7-b71d-8af61d04bdbb#1504702107","Date":1504702108.0,"errors":{"LegalRepresentativeAddress.AddressLine1":"The AddressLine1 field is required.","LegalRepresentativeAddress.City":"The City field is required.","LegalRepresentativeAddress.Country":"The Country field is required."}}`

## Workaround
Set `LegalRepresentativeAddress` to `None` if its dictionary is actually empty values.

    my_user2 = User.get(pk)
    my_user2.LegalRepresentativeAddress = None
    my_user2.save()

issue_title: Python 2.7 Support
issue_body:
I'd suggest adding python 2.7 support to enable use of this code by the wider humanitarian community.

It would be great to have `1 click` publishing of datasets to HDX from GeoNode systems (http://geonode.org/), which contain many datasets potentially useful on HDX.  For instance, a popup that appears asking if you would like to publish to HDX.

GeoNode is currently written in Python 2.7 (https://github.com/geonode/geonode).  GeoNode (as well as other systems) can only be upgraded to Python 3.0 once all their dependencies are upgraded, which could still be years away.

A standard python API that shields the complexity of CKAN's REST API is great!  However, to seamlessly integrate HDX publishing into workflows, the code will need to run directly within business systems rather than as a separate script.

issue_title: Physical interface monitoring support?
issue_body:
Hi Thomas,

Is there any support within the ExaBGP code-base for physical interface monitoring? I mean a way to shutdown a directly-connected peer BGP session if the corresponding physical interface goes down (like during a physical fiber-cut or switch/port maintenance), without waiting for the BGP session expiration timer or resorting to short TCP keepalive tricks?

It would be quite beneficial in HA/CLOS scenarii, where all servers maintain a double BGP session towards 2 different switches, and one of the switches crashes (or the attachment port is administratively shut): we would like to remove one of the 2 ECMP outgoing routes as quickly as possible, hence being notified of a "peer down" situation almost instantaneously in this case.

Thanks for your answer and sorry if this is something that's already been asked in the past.

Pierre-Yves

issue_title: Ubuntu 14.10: system-wide pip is broken by docker-py
issue_body:
My case may seem bad practice for some part of the community, but I really need to provide an access to the Docker Python API for a running docker container (e.g. mount /var/run/docker.sock into container). Our team use Ubuntu 14.10 both for development and production. So I just wanted to install the *latest* version of **docker-py** directly to system directories (using system-wide **pip**) of my Docker container. I didn't want to resort to virtualenv inside a container because it's an excess level of isolation.

Unfortunately installation of **docker-py** anywhere outside the virtualenv leads to **pip** corruption on the recent version of Ubuntu. I guess that the reason is broken backward compatibility of **requests** module.
```bash
$ docker run -it --rm ubuntu:utopic bash
$ apt-get update
$ apt-get install -y python-pip
$ pip install docker-py
$ pip freeze
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/pip", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('pip==1.5.6', 'console_scripts', 'pip')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources.py", line 356, in load_entry_point
    return get_distribution(dist).load_entry_point(group, name)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources.py", line 2476, in load_entry_point
    return ep.load()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources.py", line 2190, in load
    ['__name__'])
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pip/__init__.py", line 61, in <module>
    from pip.vcs import git, mercurial, subversion, bazaar  # noqa
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pip/vcs/mercurial.py", line 9, in <module>
    from pip.download import path_to_url
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pip/download.py", line 25, in <module>
    from requests.compat import IncompleteRead
ImportError: cannot import name IncompleteRead
```
There are a lot of workarounds here (install virtualenv inside container, etc), but probably it's possible just to reconfigure docker-py for the mentioned distribution.

is_imap function requires cpd
issue_body:
When you try to check if a given graphical model is an IMAP of a known joint probability distribution, the function "is_imap" for the "JointProbabilityDistribution" class errors if you don't give it a known CPD:

```
from pgmpy.factors.discrete import JointProbabilityDistribution

JPD1 = JointProbabilityDistribution(['I','D'],[2,2],[0.42,0.18,0.28,0.12])
JPD2 = JointProbabilityDistribution(['I','D'],[2,2],[.282,.02,.564,.134])

model1 = BayesianModel()
model1.add_node('D')
model1.add_node('I')
nx.draw_networkx(model1)
plt.show()

print 'model 1: '
print JPD1.is_imap(model1)
print JPD2.is_imap(model1)
print '-------------------'

model2 = BayesianModel([('D','I')])
nx.draw_networkx(model2)
plt.show()
print 'model 2: '
print JPD1.is_imap(model2)
print JPD2.is_imap(model2)
print '-------------------'



```

gives the error: 

```
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-57-6b88188c567c> in <module>()
     11 
     12 print 'model 1: '
---> 13 print JPD1.is_imap(model1)
     14 print JPD2.is_imap(model1)
     15 print '-------------------'

/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pgmpy-0.1.3-py2.7.egg/pgmpy/factors/discrete/JointProbabilityDistribution.pyc in is_imap(self, model)
    363             raise TypeError("model must be an instance of BayesianModel")
    364         factors = [cpd.to_factor() for cpd in model.get_cpds()]
--> 365         factor_prod = six.moves.reduce(mul, factors)
    366         JPD_fact = DiscreteFactor(self.variables, self.cardinality, self.values)
    367         if JPD_fact == factor_prod:

TypeError: reduce() of empty sequence with no initial value
```

I believe that you shouldn't need to define a cpd in order to infer if a given graph is an IMAP or not. 


I haven't tried to fix this myself, and may if I find some time, but I thought it'd be maybe something useful for ya'll to know about.

issue_title: Websocket server-side sendClose results in a connection reset instead of a graceful termination.
issue_body: When using WebSocketServerProtocol's sendClose() function, the underlying TCP connection gets reset (RST) instead of a graceful teardown (FIN/ACK). This leads to a non-clean call on the client side in WebSocketClientProtocol's connectionLost handler. This leads to messages like > Connection to the other side was lost in a non-clean fashion. To illustrate I modified the echo-example in examples/twisted/websocket. `server.py`: On the server-side, when we recieve a "CLOSE" message from the client, close the connection. ```python from autobahn.twisted.websocket import WebSocketServerProtocol, \ WebSocketServerFactory class MyServerProtocol(WebSocketServerProtocol): def onConnect(self, request): print("Client connecting: {0}".format(request.peer)) def onOpen(self): print("WebSocket connection open.") def onMessage(self, payload, isBinary): if isBinary: print("Binary message received: {0} bytes".format(len(payload))) else: print("Text message received: {0}".format(payload.decode('utf8'))) if payload.decode("utf8") == "CLOSE": self.sendClose() # echo back message verbatim self.sendMessage(payload, isBinary) def onClose(self, wasClean, code, reason): print("WebSocket connection closed: {0}".format(reason)) if __name__ == '__main__': import sys from twisted.python import log from twisted.internet import reactor log.startLogging(sys.stdout) factory = WebSocketServerFactory(u"ws://127.0.0.1:9000") factory.protocol = MyServerProtocol reactor.listenTCP(9000, factory) reactor.run() ``` `client.py`: The client is modified to send a "CLOSE" message as the second message. The factory reacts on a lost connection by either stopping the reactor (on proper close) or reconnecing (on errorous close, like in the reconnecting factory examples). Also added a bunch of debug messages. ```python from autobahn.twisted.websocket import WebSocketClientProtocol, \ WebSocketClientFactory from twisted.internet.error import ConnectionDone class MyClientFactory(WebSocketClientFactory): """Factory that terminates, when the connection is closed.""" def clientConnectionLost(self, connector, reason): print("WebSocket connection lost (in factory) on reason: {0}".format(reason)) if reason.type == ConnectionDone: # We are fine with exiting. print("Stop reactor.") self.reactor.stop() else: # Reconnect, connection dropped improperly. connector.connect() class MyClientProtocol(WebSocketClientProtocol): def onConnect(self, response): print("Server connected: {0}".format(response.peer)) def onOpen(self): print("WebSocket connection open.") self.no = 0 def hello(): self.no += 1 if self.no == 2: self.sendMessage(u"CLOSE".encode('utf8')) self.sendClose() return self.sendMessage(u"Hello, world!".encode('utf8')) self.sendMessage(b"\x00\x01\x03\x04", isBinary=True) self.factory.reactor.callLater(1, hello) # start sending messages every second .. hello() def onMessage(self, payload, isBinary): if isBinary: print("Binary message received: {0} bytes".format(len(payload))) else: print("Text message received: {0}".format(payload.decode('utf8'))) def onClose(self, wasClean, code, reason): print("WebSocket connection closed on code {1}: {0}".format(reason, code)) def connectionLost(self, reason): # Debugging only, the actual work is done in clientConnectionLost in # the factory. print("WebSocket connection lost (in protocol) on reason: {0}".format(reason)) print("Clean close:", reason.type == ConnectionDone) # Should be True, since we did it cleanly ... # Pass the event, so that 'onClose' event will be triggered. WebSocketClientProtocol.connectionLost(self, reason) if __name__ == '__main__': import sys from twisted.python import log from twisted.internet import reactor log.startLogging(sys.stdout) factory = MyClientFactory(u"ws://127.0.0.1:9000") factory.protocol = MyClientProtocol reactor.connectTCP("127.0.0.1", 9000, factory) reactor.run() print("Good bye.") ``` This creates an infinite loop in the client (closing and reestablishing the connection), since in MyClientFactory's clientConnectionLost the reason.type is ConnectionLost. Since the connection in closed properly on both sides, I think it should be ConnectionDone. I tracked this down to autobahn.websocket.protocol.WebSocketProtocol's onCloseFrame, which uses self.dropConnection(abort=True) (line 729) to close the server-side connection if it recieves a 'reply-close' frame and self.dropConnection(abort=False) (line 756) if the client initiated the close. abort=True seems to do a reset on the TCP connection, whereas abort=FALSE tears it down gracefully. Changing also to abort=False in line 729 seems to be the correct behavior.
