    descriptor='enliten-datapackage/datapackage.json',
    name="enliten-datapackage",
    backend='sql',
    engine=engine)
```
None of the resources have `name`s (now required).  This results in the `foreignKeys` generated in one of the resources in the `datapackage.json` referring to `null` resources.  

```
"schema": {
  "foreignKeys": [
    {
      "fields": "user_id",
      "reference": {
        "resource": null,
        "fields": "id"
      }
    },
    {
      "fields": "group_id",
      "reference": {
        "resource": null,
        "fields": "id"
      }
    }
  ]

```

(Found via this https://github.com/frictionlessdata/pilot-dm4t/issues/19)

issue_title: Tests folder in package directory causes installation issues
issue_body: Move tests from `<package dir>/tests` to `<package dir>/<module_dir>/tests`

issue_title: Asynchronous operations
issue_body:
As a Neutron user, I'd like a10's orchestration and scaling activities to be asynchronous so that I can continue to use the Neutron process while they are happening.

Doing this without a thread per activity will require a new `acos_client` library with greenlet support using something like [`grequests`](https://github.com/kennethreitz/grequests) and openstack clients with lightweight async support. 

Not needed if Neutron [monkey patches eventlet](http://eventlet.net/doc/patching.html#monkeypatching-the-standard-library).

issue_title: How to perform secure erase using REST API ?
issue_body:
I would like to perform the HP iLO Secure Erase using automation, instead of having to manually go into the Intelligent Provisioning area and selecting "Erase Utility".  Can you provide an example to perform this task? Or is this a question for the python-redfish-utility (https://github.com/HewlettPackard/python-redfish-utility) ? 

Thanks!

i allready use the command : "$deisctl config platform set domain=local3.deisapp.com".

issue_title: deis-builder stuck in "activating/start-post" after ExecStart failed
issue_body:
In a recent [vagrant test](https://ci.deis.io/job/test-coreos-beta/184/), builder hit an error during its `boot` script that somehow didn't restart the unit:
```console
core@deis-03 ~ $ systemctl status deis-builder
● deis-builder.service - deis-builder
   Loaded: loaded (/run/fleet/units/deis-builder.service; linked-runtime; vendor preset: disabled)
   Active: activating (start-post) (Result: exit-code) since Fri 2015-06-05 19:03:41 ; 24min ago
  Process: 2984 ExecStart=/bin/sh -c IMAGE=`/run/deis/bin/get_image /deis/builder` && docker run --name deis-builder --rm -p 2223:22 --volumes-from=deis-builder-data -c 800 -e EXTERNAL_PORT=2223 -e HOST=$COREOS_PRIVATE_IPV4 --privileged -v /etc/environment_proxy:/etc/environment_proxy $IMAGE (code=exited, status=1/FAILURE)
  Process: 2975 ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c /sbin/losetup -f (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 2962 ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c docker inspect deis-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker rm -f deis-builder || true (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 2660 ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c IMAGE=`/run/deis/bin/get_image /deis/builder` && docker history $IMAGE >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker pull $IMAGE (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
  Process: 2613 ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c docker inspect deis-builder-data >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker run --name deis-builder-data -v /var/lib/docker alpine:3.1 /bin/true (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
 Main PID: 2984 (code=exited, status=1/FAILURE);         : 2985 (sh)
   Memory: 748.0K
   CGroup: /system.slice/deis-builder.service
           └─control
             ├─2985 /bin/sh -c echo 'Waiting for builder on 2223/tcp...' && until ncat $COREOS_PRIVATE_IPV4 2223 --exec '/us...
             └─6808 sleep 1

Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2660]: 89a68d4ec32b: Pulling metadata
Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2660]: 89a68d4ec32b: Pulling fs layer
Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2660]: 89a68d4ec32b: Download complete
Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2660]: 89a68d4ec32b: Download complete
Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2660]: Status: Downloaded newer image for 10.0.1.15:5000/deis/builder:jenkins-test-coreos-beta-184
Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2975]: /dev/loop0
Jun 05 19:03:50 deis-03 sh[2985]: Waiting for builder on 2223/tcp...
Jun 05 19:04:14 deis-03 sh[2984]: etcd_safe_mkdir: an etcd error occurred (Error:  501: All the given peers are not r...d) [0])
Jun 05 19:04:14 deis-03 sh[2984]: aborting...
Jun 05 19:04:14 deis-03 systemd[1]: deis-builder.service: main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Hint: Some lines were ellipsized, use -l to show in full.

issue_title: Graceful shutdown with SIGTERM
issue_body:
On Heroku, processes are given a [10 second][2] grace period to exit following a `SIGTERM` before they are `SIGKILL`d during the standard [shutdown procedure][3]. I understand that this is the [default behavior][4] of `docker stop <id>`. 

What's the status of graceful shutdowns & restarts with deis? I'm not familiar enough with fleet to know if it is signalling containers automatically when a container [is destroyed][1]. I take it that `stop` is [not being called explicitly][5]?

[1]: https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/v1.3.1/controller/scheduler/fleet.py#L192
[2]: https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/error-codes#r12-exit-timeout
[3]: https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/dynos#shutdown
[4]: https://docs.docker.com/reference/commandline/cli/#stop
[5]: https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/v1.3.1/controller/scheduler/fleet.py#L188

issue_title: Rewrite components in Go so they are resilient, testable, and self-publishing
issue_body:
There's been some discussion and debate in #2701 about how to approach refactoring components. The core maintainers had an architectural meeting today and decided to move forward with a Go-based rewrite of the components, with the following goals:
- ~~No more Docker-in-Docker for the builder component -- instead, bind-mount the host's Docker volume so we can use that Docker graph~~ WONTFIX: This introduces security issues (breakouts in container builds can affect the host OS)
- Better log output for components, showing the component's actual status and helpful details when it fails (no more `waiting for confd to write initial templates` as in #2906)
- Components are more easily testable and less fragile (such as builder - see #3578)
- confd runs in-memory, enabling us to vendor some of its code so we can use native templating logic
- Shared Go-based libraries (with unit tests!) for code common among boot scripts
- Boot logic in a component is minimal thanks to the previous point ^
- Cleaner code and improved control flow made possible by use of Go idioms and channels (for example, rewriting the SSL certificate generation logic)

A long-term goal here is to at some point in the future be able to say `docker run -P deis/builder:2.0` and have the component come up and publish itself with everything it needs self-contained.

**Implementation details/questions**
- Do we use go-extpoints or not? An argument against is that it lends itself well to plugins, but that may not be as useful in this implementation

**Bonus**
It would be great to address the following as part of this refactor:
- nginx template rewrite (#3536)
- better handling of Ceph OSD creation errors (#2519)

replaces #2701

issue_title: `globus whoami` doesn't support `--format JSON`
issue_body:
The `--format` option is supposed to be global.

`globus whoami --format JSON` should produce a JSON record.
It can probably always be the full record -- layering `--verbose` with `--format JSON` doesn't seem terribly valuable.

This will become doubly important when we add #156, as it will be queryable.

issue_title: Enumerate and address all cases of commands where `--format TEXT` is the JSON output
issue_body:
There are a number of commands which I took shortcuts on before I had complete and working tooling for table output.
To get things working without fiddling with output too much, I used the JSON output for both output formats.

In some cases, this may ultimately be the most sensible approach (probably not).
Regardless, we should list all of these as items to address before considering the CLI "done".

issue_title: LLL: Support literals
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

This is more of a reminder for myself. No action necessary.

Literal support was not included in PR #408 as I didn't consider parsing assembly output from `lllc` - just the source and its cleaned-up tree. (PR was also getting too big.)

Detecting literals in output from `lllc -a` should be simple with some globs/regexes: they are printed at the particular place and with the specific value as where they'll appear in compiled bytecode.

It should therefore be simple to strip the bytecode of those (and only those) that appear in "deployment-only" code when generating "runtime" bytecode from regular `lllc -x` output.

#### Cute Animal Picture

![](https://i.pinimg.com/736x/95/3b/71/953b719404df6ee4633be74d9186debf.jpg)

issue_title: Accept list or single string for filter_parameter `address`
issue_body:
### What was wrong?
see: #999


### How can it be fixed?

The abi type for the filte `address` parameter was changed from `address` to `address[]`, and an additional formatting layer was added to wrap single address strings in a list. 
A new middleware was added for formatting method parameters prior to other formatting steps such as abi formatting and pythonic formatting.

issue_title: Implement LocalAccount equality test
issue_body:
## What was wrong?

### Code that produced the error

```py
from eth_account import Account
key = b'a' * 32
acct1 = Account.privateKeyToAccount(key)
acct2 = Account.privateKeyToAccount(key)
assert acct1 == acct2, "local accounts with the same private key should evaluate as equal"
```

### Full error output 

```sh
AssertionError: local accounts with the same private key should evaluate as equal
```

## How can it be fixed?

Add an `__eq__` to `LocalAccount`, test that both objects are exactly the same type, and that they have the same key.

Bonus:  Make the private key attribute of `LocalAccount` immutable, and implement `__hash__` as `hash(<private_key>)`.

Researching about this told me that the sha256 mismatch might have happened when the same package was installed from different channels into different environments. How do I get rid of this warning?

issue_title: Request for normalizing '-', '_', '.' in conda package names
issue_body:
From conda's point of view, packages with the names 'x.y', 'x_y' and 'x-y' are different. Some examples are:
```
ruamel.yaml vs ruamel_yaml
the-silver-searcher vs the_silver_searcher
repoze-lru vs repoze.lru
```

In the pip world, package names are normalized, as per: https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0503/#normalized-names

It'd be great if Conda did it too, which will make life easy, instead of accidentally clobbering files of same package but with different versions.

issue_title: Error installing python 2.7 with conda due to document saving permissions in windows
issue_body:
Due to the corporate security software installed on my computer, document files such as *.doc or *.pdf files cannot be saved.  

Using an .exe installation package of anaconda I was able to install python 3.4, however when I created a new environment and attempted to install python 2.7 I ran into trouble.  Is it possible to create my own conda package for the python 2.7 installation, which doesn't install specific *.doc or *.pdf files?  Basically I would just take the install tarball and extract the files into a folder and then edit the install scripts to avoid installation of the document files.  Is there anyone familiar with what install scripts I would have to change and where I could find references to files such as "~/lib/mdb.doc"?

Thank you!

issue_title: IPython Notebook's Windows shortcut's Start in folder sohuld be user's home
issue_body:
I'm not sure if this is the right place to report this, so please let me know if I should go to somewhere else.

Currently when I install ipython, the automatically generated shortcut for the notebook has `Start in` value `C:\Users\username\Documents\IPython Notebooks`. This forces the user to put notebooks inside that folder, since you cannot navigate outside of the folder where the kernel started at.

I'm currently teaching with Anaconda and this caused a lot of confusion to my students - they usually download a notebook to their Downloads folder and gets confused because when they open the IPython Notebook, they just get an empty window. It is not easy to explain the `Start in` folder thing to them since most of them are beginners and in my opinion it is unnecessary. The `Start in` folder should be the user's home, so they can navigate into whatever directory they want.

issue_title: binaries from sub-environ on different volume can't find shared libraries
issue_body:
When executing a binary installed in an environment created from a parent environment binaries with dependencies outside their package can't find their shared libraries.

Two examples of binaries that require libraries from other packages and are therefore broken:
* curl: requires openssl package
* ncdump (from libnetcdf): required hdf5 package

To reproduce:
```
$ which conda
/opt/anaconda/bin/conda

$ conda create -y -p ./env curl

$ source activate ./env
(/data/brucef/env)$ conda install -y curl

(/data/brucef/env)$ which curl
/data/brucef/env/bin/curl

(/data/brucef/env)$ ldd ./env/bin/curl | grep libssl
	libssl.so.1.0.0 => /data/brucef/./env/bin/../lib/libssl.so.1.0.0 (0x00007fc2af726000)

(/data/brucef/env)$ curl
curl: error while loading shared libraries: libssl.so.1.0.0: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
```

The problem seems to be that the RPATH is causing the lib loader to look for the library relative the actual binary path not the softlinked path:
```
(/data/brucef/env)$ ll ./env/bin/curl
lrwxrwxrwx 1 brucef domain users 53 Jan 11 23:17 ./env/bin/curl -> /home/brucef/.conda/envs/.pkgs/curl-7.45.0-0/bin/curl

(/data/brucef/env)$ readelf -a ./env/bin/curl | grep RPATH
 0x000000000000000f (RPATH)              Library rpath: [$ORIGIN/../lib]

(/data/brucef/env)$ LD_DEBUG=all curl
... snip ...
     23406:	 search path=/home/brucef/.conda/envs/.pkgs/curl-7.45.0-0/bin/../lib		(RPATH from file curl)
     23406:	  trying file=/home/brucef/.conda/envs/.pkgs/curl-7.45.0-0/bin/../lib/libssl.so.1.0.0
... snip ...
```

Seems like it should be using the path of the softlink `/data/brucef/env/bin/../lib/libssl.so.1.0.0`

issue_title: Misleading conda update message when conflict errors.
issue_body:

In these cases the higher version will probably have some sort of conflict, but the information provided to the user does not reflect this as a possibility, which leads to users asking. issue_title: conda uncontrollably spawning python processes when creating/removing envs
issue_body: On Windows 7, with Anaconda installed for all users in C:\Anaconda3. No problem creating and removing envs with admin rights. However, when running as user without admin rights, I get the following problem when creating environments: packages are downloaded ok, but then it hangs (usually at the linking or unlinking packages step) and I can see pythonw.exe processes being launched in task manager at a rate of several per second. Only way to stop this is to shut down the machine. The problem is intermittent and seems to occur more frequently when creating envs with all the anaconda packages (e.g. conda create -n test anaconda=4.1.1) rather than environments that only contain a few packages. Happens occasionally when trying to remove envs. I have tried reinstalling with multiple versions of anaconda (inc. the latest, 4.3.0) but the problem keeps occurring.

issue_title: Track requested packages
issue_body:
Some time back @mcg1969 , @mingwandroid and I had a discussion about tracking requested packages vs. unrequested dependencies needed to satisfy the requested ones. We agreed it would be valuable for `conda` to track requested packages as explained in this [comment]( https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/2651#issuecomment-225313705 ).

One example of a case needing to track requested packages is upgrading a Python 2 to Python 3 environment where compatibility packages (e.g. `backports.*`) are installed. We are ok with the backports that are explicitly installed being removed to accomplish an upgrade to Python 3. However, we can't currently explain this to `conda` without `conda` learning about which packages we care about being installed.

Another example I was thinking of this lately is solver priority. For instance, if I install package `A` and it pulls in `B` and `C`, when I run upgrade a downgrade for `A` could be preferred if it will result in an upgrade to `B` and `C. However, this seems counterintuitive as `A` is the package I care about having installed. So I would want the latest version of `A` always even if that means `B` and `C` get downgraded.

xref: https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/2651

issue_title: notebook broken with miniconda on windows with installer options unchecked
issue_body:
I'm getting `kernel error` when I try to create any notebook with my new miniconda3 environment on windows.  Here's what I did:
* fresh install of miniconda3 on windows 7
* installed just for me (local user)
* unchecked both boxes on the installer so my path and registry are not modified
* opened a windows command prompt
* changed directory to ~/appdata/local/continuum/miniconda3/scripts
* typed "activate"
* typed "conda install anaconda-navigator"
* typed "anaconda-navigator"  (get "libpng warning: iCCP: known incorrect sRGB profile" in command prompt window)
* clicked "install" on the notebook app

issue_title: fetch does not work properly on CRAM files when multiple_iterators is True
issue_body:
CRAM files do not apparently work properly when used with `cram.fetch(..., multiple_iterators=True)`. Using the examples from the tests/pysam_data directory and the following test code:

```
import pysam

def test_fetcher(fetcher):
    print(next(fetcher).reference_name)

chrom = "chr2"
start = 200
end = 2005

# these all work
bam = pysam.AlignmentFile("pysam_data/ex1.bam")
test_fetcher(bam.fetch(chrom, start, end))

bam = pysam.AlignmentFile("pysam_data/ex1.bam")
test_fetcher(bam.fetch(chrom, start, end, multiple_iterators=True))

cram = pysam.AlignmentFile("pysam_data/ex1.cram")
test_fetcher(cram.fetch(chrom, start, end))

# this one does not
cram = pysam.AlignmentFile("pysam_data/ex1.cram")
test_fetcher(cram.fetch(chrom, start, end, multiple_iterators=True))
```

I get the output:

```
chr2
chr2
chr2
chr1
```

Where I'd expect all the results to be chr2. It appears that the fetch on the cram file is starting from the beginning, rather than the specified coordinates.

I'm using the latest version of pysam from github. I'm not sure exactly what's going on in #293, but this may be a related issue.

issue_title: [Bug] Temporary files should be created at temporary dir
issue_body:
Reasons:
* maybe not possible to create them at the current file path (as seen below)
* if access is granted and something goes wrong you have temporary files that likely stay in a non temporary folder

```log
ERROR::pyqode.backend::something went bad with worker <function lint at 0x034C10C0>(data={'path': 'C:\\TEST.COB', 'code': [removed from log], 'encoding': 'cp1252'})
ERROR::pyqode.backend::Traceback (most recent call last):
ERROR::pyqode.backend::  File "c:\dev\pyqode.core\pyqode\core\backend\server.py", line 135, in _handle
ERROR::pyqode.backend::  File "C:\Dev\OpenCobolIDE\open_cobol_ide\linter.py", line 33, in lint
ERROR::pyqode.backend::PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: 'C:\\.oci1433411286.cbl'
```

This is what cobc/libcob does (pseudocode):

```c
if !(tmpdir = getenv ("TMPDIR")) {
#ifdef	_WIN32
   if (tmpdir = !getenv ("TEMP"))
      if !(tmpdir = getenv ("TMP"))
         if !(tmpdir = getenv ("USERPROFILE"))
            tmpdir = .;
#else
   if (tmpdir = !getenv ("TMP"))
      if !(tmpdir = getenv ("TEMP"))
         tmpdir = /tmp;
#endif

   setenv("TMPDIR", tmpdir);
}
```

For supporting relative paths to copybooks it should be enough to pass an additional -I $fullpath_of_current_file to the compiler

issue_title: Wondering how to express --timestring when month is variable length
issue_body:
Hi folks,
I have an ES cluster with monthly indices called "tracking_m_yyyy", which produces tracking_1_2016, tracking_2_2016 ... tracking_12_2016. When they're three months old, they need to get closed.
I'm struggling to get Curator to work with the way the date is expressed in the names of the indices.

If it matters, this is on a CentOS 7 box, Python is v2.7.12, Curator is v3.5.1, ES is v1.7.5.

This works, but only partially:
```
$ /usr/bin/curator --logfile /var/log/curator.log --loglevel INFO --logformat default  --host localhost --port 9200 close indices --prefix 'tracking_' --time-unit months --older-than 3 --timestring '%m_%Y'
```
The problem is the `%m` in Python's `strftime()` pads single-digit months with a zero, and my indices don't have that leading zero. 

I tried expressing `--timestring` as `%-m_%Y` instead, which works in regular Python:
```
$ python
Python 2.7.12 (default, Oct 11 2016, 05:20:59)
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.38)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import datetime
>>> datetime.datetime(2015,3,5).strftime('%m_%Y')
'03_2015'
>>> datetime.datetime(2015,3,5).strftime('%-m_%Y')
'3_2015'
```

But apparently Curator doesn't like that:
```

issue_title: Implicit HTTP port violates scheme default
issue_body:
The `elasticsearch.Elasticsearch` class treats members of the `hosts` argument list in a way that fails to conform to [RFC-3986, Section 3.2.3](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-3.2.3). A URL that does not specify a port should use the port that is the default for the _scheme_, which is "http" in this case. The scheme's default port may be overridden by a local cache of the [services](https://www.iana.org/assignments/service-names-port-numbers/service-names-port-numbers.xhtml) registry, typically `/etc/services` or `C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\services`.

### Expected behavior

```
>>> import elasticsearch
>>> elasticsearch.VERSION
(6, 1, 0, 'dev')
>>> es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch("http://localhost")
>>> es.__dict__['transport'].__dict__['seed_connections']
(<Urllib3HttpConnection: http://localhost:80>,)
>>>
```

### Actual behavior

```
>>> import elasticsearch
>>> elasticsearch.VERSION
(6, 1, 0, 'dev')
>>> es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch("http://localhost")
>>> es.__dict__['transport'].__dict__['seed_connections']
(<Urllib3HttpConnection: http://localhost:9200>,)
>>>
```

issue_title: A patch is required for Homebrew formula to support 'az extension add'
issue_body:
A patch is required for the Homebrew formula and this issue documents that patch.

Azure CLI extensions, `az extension add` use `pip install --target` in order to install extensions

`python3` on Homebrew does not support `pip install --target` [1,2,3].

A workaround is to temporarily create the file `~/.pydistutils.cfg` with contents `[install]\nprefix=`.
This file cannot stay permanently as this affects normal pip installs in Homebrew [4].
However, it's fine to attempt to do this when `az extension add` is executed on Homebrew which was what [5] achieves.

[1] [Python pip install with --target option doesn't work #837](https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/issues/837)
[2] [Fix pip install --target #4103](https://github.com/pypa/pip/pull/4103)
[3] [DistutilsOptionError: must supply either home or prefix/exec-prefix — not both](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/24257803/distutilsoptionerror-must-supply-either-home-or-prefix-exec-prefix-not-both)
[4] [Do not recommend pip install --prefix=<empty> hack, this breaks default pip command #821](https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/pull/821)
[5] https://gist.github.com/derekbekoe/a53611a1b02515b72a1047e0da268d5b

issue_title: [DNS] 'az network dns record-set <command> -h' help text format improvement
issue_body:
Consider:

```
C:\Users\jonatul>az network dns record-set create -h

Command
    az network dns record-set create: Create an empty record set within a DNS zone.
        If a matching record set already exists, it will be overwritten unless --if-none-match is
        supplied.

Arguments
    --name -n           [Required]: The name of the record set, relative to the name of the zone.
    --resource-group -g [Required]: Name of resource group.
    --type -t           [Required]: The type of DNS records in the record set.  Allowed values: A,
                                    AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, PTR, SRV, TXT.
```

For `--type -t`, the list of allowed values breaks very awkwardly, with `A` on the first line and the remaining record types on the second line. If the console window is sized to cause a line break before the `A`, it's even worse, with the `A` appearing on its own on the far left.

Can we modify the line breaking, so 'Allowed values: A, AAAA, ...etc' is all grouped together, on the second line?

This applies to all commands under `az network dns record-set`.

issue_title: [Testing] Test framework should support running in parallel
issue_body: The VCR tests currently run in series. This used to be necessary because multiple tests could reuse resource groups or other resources. These issues have been resolved and it would be beneficial if the tests executed in parallel, particularly during recording where certain long running operations can take several minutes to execute. This will be crucial when entire test suites need to be re-recorded.

issue_title: List compute Plans
issue_body:
For certain images, we are receiving an error when provisioning Virtual Machines:

    Azure Error: VMMarketplaceInvalidInput Message: Creating a virtual machine from Marketplace image requires Plan information in the request.

VirtualMachines have a [parameter for Plan](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/compute/v2016_04_30_preview/models/virtual_machine.py#L31). And I see [a model for Plan](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/compute/v2016_04_30_preview/models/plan.py), but no operations.

How can we list available plans so we know what information to pass to the VirtualMachine's create_or_update()? 
How can we know which images require a plan? It looks like the VirtualMachineImage model has a plan parameter, but does that mean there is only one valid plan for that image?
For images that require a plan, how can we see which plans are valid for that image?

issue_title: Selecting Python 2 or 3 in Windows using language_version
issue_body:
Is there a way to specify Python version for a hook portably in Windows (non-machine-specific and ideally portable to other systems as well)?

In the [Overriding Language Version](https://pre-commit.com/#overriding-language-version), it is said that `language_version` is passed on to `-p` of `virtualenv`, which enables using, for instance, `language_version: python3` in systems where there is an executable named `python3`. However, in Windows, all CPython versions are simply called `python.exe`, and there is the `py` launcher to select which Python to launch (with `py -3`, for instance). Of course, one can set the `language_version` to the full path to `python.exe`, but then pre-commit configuration becomes machine-specific.

If not possible yet, would it be possible to accept just the version number in the `language_version` for Python (like it is for Ruby) and internally resolving the path to the Python executable to pass to virtualenv?

issue_title: Python2 and Python3 with pre-commit
issue_body:
Hey!

Some of our developers (including me), have python2.7 as the default interpreter for various reasons, we code for python3.4 though, using virtualenvs this is an easy change.

However, pre-commit seems to only use the default interpreter and that can cause many various problems, such as pylint not knowing what is correct and incorrect syntax etc.

Would it be possible to initialize a pre-commit environment with a specific python version? I was thinking maybe to put it in the `.pre-commit-config.yml` in sort of manner like this:

    ---
    py_versions:
      - python2.7
      - python3.4
    ...

That way you could run the pre-commit hook for several environments (sort of like tox) or just choose a specific version. This would solve a lot of our daily gripes with pre-commit

issue_title: When using `types`, why is it necessary to specify a noop `files`?

Thanks!

issue_title: Runner should log to log files when started gauge is started as daemon
issue_body:
Runner console logs should be logged to the log file when started as daemon.
When gauge is running as lsp server, runner console should be redirected to lsp.log.
When gauge is running as api daemon, runner console should be redirected to api.log.
In the above two cases, runner should log to the log files with proper formatting. Current log file format for gauge is 'HH:MM:ss.l'. (eg :- 12:50:11.221)

When gauge is started in CLI, console should be the standard output. It should not log with timestamp.

issue_title: Normalize path middleware throws errors and doesn't work
issue_body:
## Long story short

Normalize path middleware is unusable, because it throws exceptions on every request.

## Expected behaviour

As described in the [docs](https://docs.aiohttp.org/en/stable/web_reference.html#normalize-path-middleware).

## Actual behaviour

When trying to use the middleware it throws a TypeError
```
Error handling request
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Git\_temp\.venv\Lib\site-packages\aiohttp\web_protocol.py", line 381, in start
    resp = await self._request_handler(request)
  File "C:\Git\_temp\.venv\Lib\site-packages\aiohttp\web_app.py", line 308, in _handle
    handler = await m(app, handler)
TypeError: normalize_path_middleware() takes 0 positional arguments but 2 were given
```


## Steps to reproduce

1. Start application:
```python
from aiohttp import web

async def hello(request):
    return web.Response(text="Hello, world")

app = web.Application(
    middlewares=[web.normalize_path_middleware]
)
app.router.add_get('/hello/', hello)

web.run_app(app)
```

2. Go to http://localhost:8080/hello


## Your environment

*
  * Windows 10 Pro x64
  * Python 3.6.4 (v3.6.4:d48eceb, Dec 19 2017, 06:54:40) [MSC v.1900 64 bit (AMD64)]
  * aiohttp 3.0.9 (same behaviour for 2.3.4).
*
  * Docker image `python:3.6-slim-stretch`
  * Python 3.6.4 (default, Mar 14 2018, 17:46:38)
  * aiohttp 3.0.9.

issue_title: Systemwide text navigation shortcuts ignored in notebook edit mode
issue_body:
Hi all,

I'm having a problem where some of the text navigation shortcuts I use in all other applications and contexts in macOS (High Sierra) are not reflected in a Jupyter notebook's edit mode. 

To provide an example, typically to move the cursor to the previous word in any application (including Safari or Chrome) you would use the keyboard shortcut "alt+left arrow key." However, I prefer to change this to "command+left arrow key" using some keybinding voodoo as outlined [here](http://benogle.com/2010/01/18/windowslinux-developers-remap-your-mac.html). This works great in every aspect of the OS and all other apps, but not in a Jupyter notebook's edit mode. For some reason Jupyter does not respect the systemwide text navigation shortcuts, and instead defaults to "alt + left arrow key". 

It's obviously a small thing but drives me a bit nuts because I switch between various data analysis packages often and it's a bit jarring. Any help would be helpful!

issue_title: Packaging - the custom msg, entry points, and cached static assets solution.
issue_body:
Since we haven't agreed on this yet, I'm opening an issue instead of writing an IPEP.  If this is agreed on, I'll write an IPEP.

The week before last, @ellisonbg and I brainstormed about Jupyter packaging.  As I remember it, the best solution we came up with requires a combination of Python packaging entry-points, a new message type, and static asset caching (in the web server).  This is my understanding of how this solution would work (my notes from our meeting are at home, and my apartment is being fumigated, so I don't have access to them).
## Jupyter level, kernel extensions

![screen shot 2015-05-26 at 1 47 13 pm](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3292874/7822986/f5dba636-03ad-11e5-8de4-8d559bd46acb.png)
A new message, the first, (in blue) would be added, allowing the server to ask the kernel if the static assets it knows about, associated with that kernel, is correct.  The message would be a dict of static asset path and contents hashes.

The same message in the opposite direction is the kernel's response.  It would be some type of data structure, maybe a binary message, containing static asset paths and their contents, and a list of the static assets that can be deleted from the cache.

A second new message (in red), would be added that would allow the kernel to invoke a require.js call in the front-end.  This is preferred over standard display(JS) calls, because the notebook contents will remain unaffected.
## IPython level, kernel extensions

Python entry points will be used as a registry.  Two entry points will be defined:  
1. an entry point for code to run when the kernel is started.
2. an entry point for a method that returns static assets (paths).
## Jupyter level, server extensions and notebook extensions

Python entry points will be used as a registry.  Three entry points will be defined:  
1. an entry point for code to run when the server is started.
2. an entry point for a method that returns static assets (paths).
3. an entry point for a paths to be `require`ed when the notebook page loads.

**EDIT**
To help the discussion, issues and specific cases are listed here: https://jupyter.hackpad.com/Packaging-PbIgxnC71or

issue_title: Speed up uploading many files
issue_body:
A user reported uploading 3100 files took 2 hours.
A zipped version of these files took less than a minute.

**Current Implementation Profiling**
Profiling a smaller example of 12 folders and 17 files took **45 seconds**.
35 seconds was on the main process waiting on HTTP requests.
Creating the project took 1.2 seconds.
Each folder took average of 0.4 seconds.
Each file took average of 1.7 seconds(POST uploads: 0.6, PUT uploads_complete 0.75, files: 0.3s).
Creating the upload url and and sending content to swift store are handled on another process.

**Persistent Connection Profiling**
requests has support for using urllib3 persistent connections: http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/advanced/
Profiling the same 12 folders and 17 files took **28 seconds**.
18 seconds are spent waiting on HTTP requests.
Creating the project took 0.1 seconds.
Each folder took average of 0.1 seconds.
Each file took average of 0.9 seconds(POST uploads: 0.3, PUT uploads_complete 0.5, files: 0.1s).
Even with this improvements I estimate it would still take an hour to upload 3100 files.

**Other Ideas**
Uploading multiple files at once.
Changes to DukeDS API for batch file creation.

issue_title: global base path for shed_upload include relative paths
issue_body:
I'm trying to replace a manual process for preparing tar-balls to push to the Tool Shed, however by using a common top level ``test-data/`` (and ``tool-data/``) folder this is harder than expected.

Current manual process as documented in this tool's README file:

```
$ tar -czf venn_list.tar.gz tools/venn_list/README.rst tools/venn_list/venn_list.* tools/venn_list/tool_dependencies.xml test-data/magic.pdf test-data/venn_list.tabular test-data/rhodopsin_proteins.fasta
$ tar -tzf venn_list.tar.gz
tools/venn_list/README.rst
tools/venn_list/venn_list.py
tools/venn_list/venn_list.xml
tools/venn_list/tool_dependencies.xml
test-data/magic.pdf
test-data/venn_list.tabular
test-data/rhodopsin_proteins.fasta
```

I have been able to get the desired output (ignoring the order, #159) with a complicated ``.shed.yml`` entry like this:

```
include:
- source: README.rst
  destination: tools/venn_list/README.rst
- source: venn_list.py
  destination: tools/venn_list/venn_list.py
- source: venn_list.xml
  destination: tools/venn_list/venn_list.xml
- source: tool_dependencies.xml
  destination: tools/venn_list/tool_dependencies.xml
- source: ../../test-data/magic.pdf
  strip_components: 2
- source: ../../test-data/venn_list.tabular
  strip_components: 2
- source: ../../test-data/rhodopsin_proteins.fasta
  strip_components: 2
```

Giving:

```
$ planemo shed_upload --tar_only  ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list 
cp /tmp/tmpybJqwL shed_upload.tar.gz
(.venv)[galaxy@ppserver planemo]$ tar -tzf /tmp/tmpybJqwL
tools/
tools/venn_list/
tools/venn_list/venn_list.py
tools/venn_list/tool_dependencies.xml
tools/venn_list/README.rst
tools/venn_list/venn_list.xml
test-data/
test-data/rhodopsin_proteins.fasta
test-data/magic.pdf
test-data/venn_list.tabular
```

I would like to use something along these lines in the ``.shed.yml`` file:

```
#All include paths from two levels up (i.e. prefix with ../../ to find the file)
include_offset: 2
include:
- tools/venn_list/README.rst
- tools/venn_list/venn_list.py
- tools/venn_list/venn_list.xml
- tools/venn_list/tool_dependencies.xml
- test-data/magic.pdf
- test-data/venn_list.tabular
- test-data/rhodopsin_proteins.fasta
```

issue_title: Add rule to exclude a path, like the inverse of `pick`
issue_body:
Feature request: In cases where there are lots of directories and files to include from a dependency module _except one_, it would be nice to be able to specify explicit paths to omit completely, rather than using `pick` to verbosely declare everything to include.

I would suggest either:

1. Add an `omit` or `exclude` keyword for use in rule definitions
2. Support exclusion rules as part of `pick` statements with bangs, e.g. `pick: ! hey/neat/this/could/also/support/globbing/**`

I think I prefer 2.

issue_title: Idea: make public bitcoin disk snapshot available to community
Subdomain Registrar: Names aren't batched efficiently
issue_body:
After initial startup and queueing up a large list of names, zonefiles will start with only 1 subdomain TXT record. Each time a zonefile gets created, the number of subdomain TXT records is incremented by 1 (perhaps until the 4kb limit, but I haven’t pushed it that far yet). However, I expect each zonefile to be as close to the 4kb limit as possible, right off the bat.

Steps to reproduce:
```# Kick off the registrar scenario
docker run -dt -v /tmp/bs:/tmp quay.io/blockstack/integrationtests:develop blockstack-test-scenario --interactive 2 blockstack_integration_tests.scenarios.subdomain_registrar

# Wait for the test to complete
docker logs -f <container id> | grep inished

# Hop in
docker exec -it <container id> bash

# All the following are executed w/in the docker container
# Set env vars properly
export BLOCKSTACK_TEST=1
export BLOCKSTACK_TESTNET=1
export BLOCKSTACK_DEBUG=1
export BLOCKSTACK_CLIENT_CONFIG=/tmp/blockstack-run-scenario.blockstack_integration_tests.scenarios.subdomain_registrar/client/client.ini

# Create a bunch of subdomain registration requests
for i in {1..100}; do
  curl -X POST --data '{"zonefile": "$ORIGIN foo'"$i"'\n$TTL 3600\n_file URI 10 1 \"file:///tmp/foo'"$i"'.profile.json\"\n", "name": "foo'"$i"'", "owner_address": "14x2EMRz1gf16UzGbxZh2c6sJg4A8wcHLD"}' http://localhost:7103/register/
done

# Observe the zonefiles as they start getting registered
blockstack list_zonefile_history foo.id
```

This has to do with a few things. First, the `entries_per_tx_hint` variable, which controls how many queued registrations are queried from the database, is being set after ever batch of registrations, regardless of why the batch was a certain size. It makes sense to save the hint if a batch was scaled down due to size constrictions. However, when the size was low due to a low queue volume, the hint shouldn't be saved. The other is that the logic to increase the number of entries in each batch is spread out over multiple batches (one more each batch), instead of packing in the most zonefiles it can in a single run.

Here's a pseudo-code sketch of how I imagine this logic should work:
```
global zonefile_limit = 4096
global entries_per_tx_hint = 24
global domain = foo.id

function maximizeSubdomains() {
  rows = getQueuedRegistrations(entries_per_tx_hint);
  local zf_init = createZonefile(domain);
  local txtRecords = rows.map { row =>
  }
}
```

When batching queued subdomain requests, I'd expect it to work something like:
1. Query for `entries_per_tx_hint` number of rows (initial value is 24)
1. Try to create a zonefile with all results.
1. If zonefile is too big:
   1. Remove zonefiles and check size until we're under the limit. This could be simple (`-1` each time) or smart (determine size of each TXT entry and calculate how many you need to remove in one shot)
   1. update the `entries_per_tx_hint` with resulting number of rows
1. If zonefile is significantly less than our limit
   1. Query more rows
   2. Go through the above process
   3. Update `entries_per_tx_hint` with the number of rows we settle on

If step 1 returns less than `entries_per_tx_hint` and they can all fit in the zonefile, don't update `entries_per_tx_hint`

issue_title: Make vg options more configurable

Many vg parameters are hardcoded in the pipeline.  Random example: https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil-vg/blob/master/src/toil_vg/vg_map.py#L175-L176

Some people will want/need to change the various options.  The approach in tvg_call.py, passing in '--call_opts' etc. as a string, is pretty rough and won't be fun to scale to the whole pipeline.

issue_title: BatchRunner's "variable parameters" is not strictly optional
issue_body:
I was trying to use `BatchRunner` to run multiple iterations of my model that had only fixed parameter sets. Initially, when reading the [docs](http://mesa.readthedocs.io/en/master/apis/batchrunner.html), I saw that the `variable_parameters` is specified as an optional keyword argument. However, if I do not pass in any variable parameters, then BatchRunner fails with the error:

```
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-54-793336723b8d> in <module>()
      8                         iterations=10,
      9                         max_steps=12 * 20,
---> 10                         model_reporters={'Projects': stage_counts})
     11 batch_run.run_all()

~/anaconda/envs/prog_forecast/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mesa/batchrunner.py in __init__(self, model_cls, variable_parameters, fixed_parameters, iterations, max_steps, model_reporters, agent_reporters, display_progress)
     81         """
     82         self.model_cls = model_cls
---> 83         self.variable_parameters = self._process_parameters(variable_parameters)
     84         self.fixed_parameters = fixed_parameters or {}
     85         self._include_fixed = len(self.fixed_parameters.keys()) > 0

~/anaconda/envs/prog_forecast/lib/python3.6/site-packages/mesa/batchrunner.py in _process_parameters(self, params)
    101         params = copy.deepcopy(params)
    102         bad_names = []
--> 103         for name, values in params.items():
    104             if (isinstance(values, str) or
    105                     not hasattr(values, "__iter__")):

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'items'
```

I felt confused when I first encountered this error, as I was expecting `BatchRunner` to gracefully handle the `variable_parameters=None` scenario. It thus appeared as a bug to me. 

If this is truly a bug, I think there's two different ways fixing this could go. If the intent is to make `variable_parameters` an optional keyword argument, then adding code to deal with its optionality would be good, e.g. some conditionals that deal with it. Otherwise, if the intent is to make `variable_parameters` a non-optional argument, it should not have a default value of `None`. 

May I ask which of these design choices is the intended one? If it's the latter, I'm happy to put in a PR to make it non-optional. If it's the former, then I'd like to ask for guidance on putting in a PR, as there's probably a few places I'll need to add in conditionals that I wouldn't be fully aware of.

issue_title: The example in documentation doesn't work
issue_body:
In particular, it's not possible to call `event_loop.run_until_complete()`: it raises `RuntimeError: This event loop is already running`.

Example:

``` python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import pytest
import asyncio

from aiokafka import AIOKafkaConsumer
from aiokafka.structs import TopicPartition
from async_timeout import timeout

from protopy_test.fixtures import kafka_config


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_matching_trades_proto(kafka_config, event_loop):
    topic = kafka_config.test_topic()
    servers = kafka_config.bootstrap_servers('raw')
    partition = TopicPartition(topic, 0)

    async def do_test():
        consumer = AIOKafkaConsumer(loop=event_loop, bootstrap_servers=servers)
        await consumer.start()
        consumer.assign([partition])
        await consumer.seek_to_beginning(partition)

        with timeout(10):
            data = await consumer.getmany(partition, max_records=100)
            print(data)

    event_loop.run_until_complete(do_test())
```

In order to run this, you'd need to implement `kafka_config` in such a way that `bootstrap_servers('raw')` produces a list of Kafka brokers in a form `"ip:port,ip:port,..."` and `test_topic()` is a topic with some messages already in it.  However, this isn't really important since you will never even get to interact with Kafka, the test will fail at the call to `run_until_complete()`.

[See line 268 of `oscar.forms.widgets`](https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/forms/widgets.py#L268) showing how constructor can take disabled values.

That class redefines the method "`render_option`" in order to inject the markup ` disabled="disabled"` on any values present in `self.disabled_values`.

In [Django 1.11 Release Notes line 612](https://github.com/django/django/blob/bc95314ca6af0b5e993ae07fdc7d8e6166d3b8ca/docs/releases/1.11.txt#L612), looks like the `render_option` method has been removed, which I suspect might be why I cannot have `self.disabled_values` take effect.

issue_title: Issue with similar category names
issue_body:
With ElasticSearch backend arises problem with product queryset in categories.

Let's say you have category structure like this:

- Accessories
  - Gloves
  - Sport gloves

When we add new products to "Sport gloves" they showed up also in "Gloves".

Issue causes this code:
```python
# oscar/apps/catalogue/search_handlers.py
# 72..77 lines
    def get_search_queryset(self):
        sqs = super(ESProductSearchHandler, self).get_search_queryset()
        if self.categories:
            for category in self.categories:
                sqs = sqs.filter_or(category=category.full_name)
        return sqs
```
```python
# oscar/apps/search/search_indexes.py
# 48..51 lines
    def prepare_category(self, obj):
        categories = obj.categories.all()
        if len(categories) > 0:
            return [category.full_name for category in categories]
```

Oscar use `category.full_name` for filtering products. But when we have similar names like "category" and "category something-else" whitespace causes incorrect results.

According to Haystack warning:

> Also, if a string with one or more spaces in it is specified as the value, the string will get passed along AS IS. This will mean that it will NOT be treated as a phrase (like Haystack 1.X’s behavior).
> If you want to match a phrase, you should use either the __exact filter type or the Exact input type (Input Types).

we need to change `sqs = sqs.filter_or(category=category.full_name)` to 
`sqs = sqs.filter_or(category__exact=category.full_name)` 
or
`sqs = sqs.filter_or(category=Exact(category.full_name))`

But this doesn't help.
I changed `category.full_name` to `category.pk` in this files and seems that it helps.
But maybe I missed something.

issue_title: Input validation error in BenefitForm
issue_body:
### Issue Summary

By defining a custom incentive, the input validation of a the offer creation allows to input values that lead to an error. 
After this, the whole offers page of the dashboard is inaccessible, because of an error message.

### Steps to Reproduce

Prerequisite: Existing custom incentive

1. Start creating a new offer in the dashboard
2. Set random name, e.g. "Test"
3. For the incentive just select "Range" with any of the provided options and leave the rest blank
4. Submit the incentive
5. Fill the rest of the procedure with any data
6. Submit the offer
7. Dashboard shows an error, and offers page is no longer accessible without an error

### Technical details
Usage of sandbox version as described in the [docs][sandbox]. No further modifications needed.

```
$ python --version
Python 3.6.3
$ pip show django | grep Version
Version: 2.0.6
$ pip show django-oscar | grep Version
Version: 2.0.dev0
```

The problem in this case, is that [clean][clean-method] method of the BenefitForm, does only check if one of the values is set. By setting the range, this check is fulfilled.


[sandbox]: https://django-oscar.readthedocs.io/en/latest/internals/sandbox.html#running-the-sandbox-locally
[clean-method]: https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/06323399b0dfdcbbf63606a3607ca9648a991cca/src/oscar/apps/dashboard/offers/forms.py#L137

issue_title: VS 2015 not installing dependencies - rc2 - Windows 7
issue_body:
Running a project the node_modules folder is showing things are installed (I even did an `npm i` as well), but VS thinks they aren't installed.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2574412/16243885/5d4d889a-37c7-11e6-96c2-34de53b04bb8.png)


Also when running the project, it throws internal server errors for the same reason (saying things aren't installed)

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2574412/16243896/711b1a90-37c7-11e6-93c9-c5d27c4c8a9e.png)

Is this a Windows 7 issue, or potentially something I set up wrong when going from rc1 to rc2?

Running Core 2.0 Self-Containing App on Linux fails
issue_body:
# Running a Net Core 2.0 Self-Containing App on Linux fails

Running a published self-containing app on linux results in an error regarding the assemblys manifest.
Worked well with Core 1.1.

# Investigations so far and how to reproduce

I recently converted a Net Core app 1.1 which worked fine to Net Core 2.0 and ran into an issue after publish and run at linux.
I am using Linux Pepermint8 and Ubuntu 16.04. Both showing the same errors.

"Unhandled Exception: System.IO.FileLoadException: Could not load file or assembly 'System.Console, Version=4.1.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'. The located assembly's manifest definition does not match the assembly reference. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80131040)
Aborted "

Then I decided to create a simple project on Visual Studio 2017.3 using the C# Net Core Console template with Net Core 2.0.

To make the sample self-containing, I've modified the Porject which now looks like:

```
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
    <RuntimeIdentifiers>win10-x64;linux-x64</RuntimeIdentifiers>
  </PropertyGroup>
  
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.All" Version="2.0.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

```
The main moutine is the default hello world to console output.
Nothing else changed from the default template.

Called Publish form the Solution Explorer and selected "linux-x64" as target platform.
Copied the output to my linux machines and got the same error again.
"Unhandled Exception: System.IO.FileLoadException: Could not load file or assembly 'System.Console, Version=4.1.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a'. The located assembly's manifest definition does not match the assembly reference. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80131040)
Aborted "

The deps file only references System.Console 4.3.0.

issue_title: Failure to detect webroot in CentOS7
issue_body:
In CentOS7 (and presumably other RPM/RHEL-based systems), the tool fails to detect the Apache installation on the system, which I believe to be due to it looking for the install at /etc/apache2, instead of the Cent/RHEL default of /etc/httpd.

The directory layout, for reference, on my Cent box is:

[root@drop3 ~]# find /etc/httpd/ -type d
/etc/httpd/
/etc/httpd/conf
/etc/httpd/conf.d
/etc/httpd/conf.modules.d

the conf folder holds httpd.conf (global).
conf.d holds vhost or module-specific configs
conf.modules.d holds LoadModule statements.


Kuba created #1092 as a byproduct of this w.r.t. a "bad" failure message here.

Happy to test any fixes, and I'm going to look into it myself, as well.

issue_title: Please, make Let's Encrypt client less intrusive
issue_body:
I think we can agree on that reducing the attack surface should be a principle everyone should follow. That means install the least amount of packages that still lets you get things done. Now if I do a mere

    # letsencrypt-auto --help

It installs the following packages:

    python-devel python-virtualenv gcc libffi-devel openssl-devel redhat-rpm-config cpp dwz glibc-devel glibc-headers kernel-headers keyutils-libs-devel krb5-devel libcom_err-devel libmpc libselinux-devel libsepol-devel libverto-devel mpfr pcre-devel perl-srpm-macros zip zlib-devel

**without even asking**. `yum -y` all the way. If I do this as a sudoer _user_, it still becomes root in the blink of an eye, everything's run with `sudo`.

If you think it's acceptable to install compilers and a lot of libs and headers on each and every web server for... running a Python script, that's totally up to you. But please, at least ask us if we really want that.

Now, this wouldn't bother me this much if there was a way to set up a single, isolated VM which I could use to request certificates for all our servers. Except I, of course, can't do that. The script needs to be run on the server that'll serve those domains.

I personnaly erased all these packages and reverted to our Comodo certs for now and by the look of things, it'll stay that way. I want to make it clear that I'm not _complaing_ - it wouldn't even make any sense since you're giving away certs for free, which is awesome. This is merely a suggestion that you may or may not consider.

I do believe that most admins would greatly appreciate if letsencrypt came in the way of

a) a single binary - IIRC there are tools for doing that in Python but FIXME
b) a self-contained Python script - e.g. does serving HTTP requests, amongst other, mostly simple things really require kernel headers and a C compiler in Python? I doubt it.

iinstead of a script that

a) installs a lot of bloat on web servers
b) sets up a Python virtual environment... somewhere, that may be trivial for you python guys but not for the average nginx admin and honestly, I really don't have the interest or desire to deal with it
c) installs packages via pip

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: standalone should allow for alternate ports to bind to (but not authenticate to)
issue_body:
If the `standalone` plugin were to allow users to specify which port to bind to (such as 8080), then it could be run as-needed for `certonly` behavior behind nginx/apache/ or any other server via a proxypass directive.

all challenges should would still be routed through port 80 (and 443 if needed).  this would just afford the person who owns root privileges some flexibility as to how they route traffic.

issue_title: Schema fields with dict attribute names return dict attributes when keys missing
issue_body:
Whenever a `Schema` is defined that has fields that shadow `dict` attributes (e.g. "items", "keys", "values", etc), passing a `dict` with those keys not present to `Schema.dump()` results in `dict` attributes being serialized:

```python
from marshmallow import Schema, fields

class A(Schema):
    a = fields.Str()
    keys = fields.Str(default='k')
    values = fields.Str(missing='v')
    items = fields.Str()

# No problem on load.
loaded = A().load({'a': 'x'})
print(loaded.data)
# {'a': 'x', 'values': 'v'}

# No problem when fields present.
dumped = A().dump({'a': 'x', 'keys': 'k', 'values': 'v', 'items': 'i'})
print(dumped.data)
# {'a': 'x', 'keys': 'k', 'items': 'i', 'values': 'v'}

# Undesired attribute access when fields not present.
dumped = A().dump({'a': 'x'})
print(dumped.data)

# on marshmallow==2.13.5
# {'a': 'x',
#  'keys': "dict_keys(['a'])",
#  'values': "dict_values(['x'])",
#  'items': "dict_items([('a', 'x')])"}

# on marshmallow==3.0.0b2
# {'a': 'x',
#  'keys': '<built-in method keys of dict object at 0x7f4e206bc948>'}
#  'values': '<built-in method values of dict object at 0x7f4e206bc948>',
#  'items': '<built-in method items of dict object at 0x7f4e206bc948>',
```

I found #30 which indicates that this is intended behavior for dumping `dict` objects, but I was wondering if `3.0.0` may be open to changing it (for `dict` classes only) or providing an option to disable dict-attribute access when dumping.

I'm aware that I could change the attribute name in the `Schema` definition and utilize `load_from`/`dump_to` but I wanted to avoid aliasing to keep the `Schema` attributes the same as the de-/serialization format.

In my own application, I've overridden `Schema.get_attribute` with a custom implementation so that `dict` attributes aren't accessed at all but it would be nice to not to have to do this in each project.

If overriding `Schema.get_attribute` is the preferred method for implementing this functionality (instead of having a first-class option/setting), then I'll keep doing that. However, it may be worth adding a warning or note in the documentation about this "gotcha" when dumping a `dict` object with a `Schema` that mirrors `dict` attributes. The section on [Specifying Attribute Names](http://marshmallow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart.html#specifying-attribute-names) does mention object attribute access but might be helpful to call out the potential field/dict-attribute conflicts that can result when dumping (unless I'm missing something else in the docs that covers this scenario).

Thoughts?

issue_title: Using config in automain without explicitly passing each variable
issue_body:
I'm having a problem passing config variables directly into the main function, without having to manually define each input to the main function à la: main(batch_size, num_batches, ....).

The config code, main function code (the start of it), and the returned error are defined below.
I've tried rewriting the main function as a sub-function with @ex.capture, but that gave the same error.


Define config:

```
@ex.config
def config():
    # Define parameters:
    batch_size = 25  # Number of episodes to run before updating net parameters
    num_batches = 10000
    gamma = 0.8  # Discount factor when accumulating future rewards
    optimizer = 'Adam'

    optimizer_params['lr'] = 1e-4  # Learning rate used to scale gradient updates

Run main code:

```
@ex.automain
def main():
    mode = 'train'
    # Network initialisation:
    agent = Net()  # Define agent as an object of class Net
    opt = getattr(optim,optimizer)(agent.parameters(), **optimizer_params)

issue_title: Read named arguments from a file, SCM or HTTP(S) endpoint
issue_body:
For appliances it is more common to use someone else's senza template and end up providing your own parameters.

Sometimes it could make more sense to just feed the values for those parameters directly from a file or directly from some Git or HTTP endpoint.

For ex., to use the stups-cassandra appliance, directly from https://github.com/zalando/stups-cassandra, one could write a personal stups-cassandra-_myteam_.yaml with parameter values specific to _myteam_:

    EtcdDomain: etcd.myteam.example.com
    ScalyrKey: myteam-specific-scalyr-api-key
    ApplicationId: myteam-stups-cassandra-according-to-yourturn

Such parameter files could be used with a special notation like:

    senza create definition.yaml version @stups-cassandra-myteam.yaml

or

    senza create --params stups-cassandra-myteam.yaml definition.yaml version

Along with https://github.com/zalando-stups/senza/issues/99 this could even become something like:

    senza create \
        --params https://github.com/myself/project/blob/master/stups-cassandra-myteam.yaml \
        https://github.com/zalando/stups-cassandra/blob/master/stups-cassandra.yaml \
        cluster1

(assert-soft A :weight 1 :id goal)
(assert-soft B :weight 1 :id goal)

;(minimize goal)
(maximize x)

(check-sat)
(get-objectives)
(get-model)
```

**output**:

```
sat
(objectives
 (goal 1)
 (x 200)
)
(model 
  (define-fun A () Bool
    false)
  (define-fun B () Bool
    false)
  (define-fun x () Int
    200)
)
```

Objective `x` should not evaluate `200`, because `goal` equals to `1` means that either `A` or `B` is true, which means that there should be an additional upper-bound over `x` equal to `50` or `99`.

issue_title: Make get_params optionally return expressions?
issue_body:
While implementing a form of [BinaryConnect](https://github.com/MatthieuCourbariaux/BinaryConnect) I noticed that get_params returns only the shared variables, but not expressions. That makes perfect sense e.g. for low-rank dense layers (where W = UV is a Theano expression with U and V as shared variables). I now have the opposite use case: I'd like to register an expression as a parameter of which I later want to compute the gradient, so I want get_param to return that expression instead of the shared variables contained. In principle that is easy to implement: one could add a keyword "only_shared=True" in get_params and modify the return statement. 

If you like the idea I can open a pull request.

[Further info on use case]: My use case is as follows: I have a parameter that is binary (e.g. 0 or 1), but I'd still like to use SGD to optimize that parameter. To this end I follow BinaryConnect and define a new real-valued hidden variable *p* with support *[0,1]* (through clipping). The binary parameter is then implemented as a simple Bernoulli process with probability *p*. The parameter *p* is then updated in the direction of the gradient with respect to the binary parameter (so e.g. if the binary parameter b was 1, and the is positive with respect to this parameter, then p is increased).

issue_title: `CustomRecurrentLayer` does not allow hidden-to-hidden sub-net to have arbitrary depth
issue_body:
When using a `CustomRecurrentLayer` the hidden-to-hidden network can only have one layer besides the input, unless the second last layer has the same output dimensions as output shape. 

PR #778 addresses this problem.

Here is some code based on the unit tests that triggers the bug:

```py
    # Check that the custom recurrent layer can handle a hidden-to-hidden
    # network with an arbitrary depth
    n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height = (2, 3, 4, 5, 6)
    n_out_filters = 7
    n_in_hid_filters_0 = 11
    n_hid_hid_filters_0 = 13
    filter_shape = (3, 3)
    l_in = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(
        (n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height))

    # Expect the output shape of `l_in` as input shape for input-to-hidden
    l_in_to_hid = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((None, n_channels, width, height))
    # Two conv layers; first to `n_hid_filters_0` channels
    l_in_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_in_to_hid, n_in_hid_filters_0, filter_shape, pad='same')
    # then to `n_out_filters` channels
    l_in_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_in_to_hid, n_out_filters, filter_shape, pad='same')

    # Expect the output shape of `l_in_to_hid` as input shape for
    # hidden-to-hidden
    l_hid_to_hid = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((None, n_out_filters,
                                              width, height))
    # Two conv layers; first to `n_hid_hid_filters_0` channels
    l_hid_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_hid_to_hid, n_hid_hid_filters_0, filter_shape, pad='same')
    # then to `n_out_filters` channels
    l_hid_to_hid = lasagne.layers.Conv2DLayer(
        l_hid_to_hid, n_out_filters, filter_shape, pad='same')

    # --- ValueError raised HERE ---
    l_rec = lasagne.layers.CustomRecurrentLayer(
        l_in, l_in_to_hid, l_hid_to_hid)

    assert l_rec.output_shape == (n_batch, n_steps, n_out_filters, width,
                                  height)

    out = theano.function([l_in.input_var], lasagne.layers.get_output(l_rec))
    out_shape = out(np.zeros((n_batch, n_steps, n_channels, width, height),
                             dtype=theano.config.floatX)).shape
    assert out_shape == (n_batch, n_steps, n_out_filters, width, height)
```

issue_title: `resolve_symbol` cannot resolve symbols from a library that the current module does not depend on
issue_body:
Consider the following case: Binary A depends on two libraries B and C. C provides a function `foo` that both A and B import. C is in A's library dependent list, but not in B's (i.e. `readelf -d B` does not contain C at all).

In this case, `ld` is able to load binary A with libraries B and C, and correctly resolve `foo` from both A and B to some address in C. Cle, instead, only resolves `foo` for A but not for `B` as it does not see `C` as a dependent of `B`.

To resolve this issue, we need to see how `ld` handles it internally.

issue_title: ncclient waits for a server's hello message
issue_body:
Correct me if I'm wrong, but according to [spec](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6241#section-8.1) server and client don't have to wait for each other's hello messages that include their capabilities:

> Capabilities are advertised in messages sent by each peer during
   session establishment.  When the NETCONF session is opened, each peer
   (both client and server) MUST send a <hello> element containing a
   list of that peer's capabilities.
> ...
> Each peer sends its <hello> element simultaneously as soon as the
   connection is open.  A peer MUST NOT wait to receive the capability
   set from the other side before sending its own set.

Right now, it looks like ncclient is waiting for a server to send a ```hello``` message first which clearly violates the spec.

issue_title: In-Memory cache fills up indefinitely by default
issue_body: Right now, the in-memory cache only ever gets fuller. When pulling a lot of data from the API, this can start to be a problem. I'd propose either explicitly mentioning this and how to reset the cache in documentation, or (ideally) having a maximum size for the cache / maximum number of stored objects, with some expulsion policy (LRU?), and a sane default value to start off with (but this might be hard to do properly).

issue_title: Improvements to test skipping logic
issue_body:
Redmine contains a "target platform release" field. Smash's `bug_is_untestable` logic should be aware of this field so bugs that have been moved to `ON_QA` or `VERFIED` are not run on older versions of Pulp.

An additional improvement in this area would be to get a dump of all bugs that are now testable and their target platform release to avoid making another http request to redmine for each bug.

There are some potential issues with this method. First are bugs that are fixed in an older Z release. For example, a bug that is fixed in 2.7.3 and 2.8.1. For the example lets say that 2.7.3 is released before 2.8.1. We then know that bugfixes in 2.7.3 are also available in 2.8.1. We should be able to accurately determine whether a bugfix is present by knowing the target release and the order of the releases. 

A second option would be to use `--contains` on the commit. I think that this would require less logic and could potentially be more accurate (and would work for backports), but would require very strict book keeping in redmine, specifically making sure that every bug has a commit. We would need to explore what is necessary when there are more than 1 commit with this model.

issue_title: Consider "stable" as a stable branch pattern by default
issue_body: If `CONAN_STABLE_BRANCH_PATTERN` environment variable is not set, then package tools treats `master` as the default stable branch pattern.  This is a request to treat both `master` and `stable` as default stable branch patterns.  `stable` is the currently promoted convention, and we'll be publishing over 100 packages using this as the branch name.  It would be cleaner not to have to add this environment variable to each recipe or each CI project, since there's already default logic in place.

<!-- mark with x between the [ ] -->

- [x] searched open and closed issues for duplicates

Hey guys, 

I have an Epson TM88-III hooked up to a raspberry pi through serial connection (baudrate 9600).

Everything works fine, but the printing of images & QR codes (which are basically images in non-native mode) is very slow with me. Also, I can hear the printer printing the image line by line, with quite some time in between, which I hope is not expected behaviour.

At first I thought this might have to do with time needed for data transfer, but even a 300byte image (which should take <0,5 second to transfer @ 9600 baud) gets printed 'line by line' rather slowly. 

I have made a short video how a QR code currently prints with me:

https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/u/4651651/2017-08-15%2016.05.26.mov

issue_title: CSV UTF-8 encoding
issue_body:
Hi!

First of great package, thank you!

I  encountered a problem with csv UTF-8 encoding when converting a xlsx file to csv.

If I follow the recommended way from the docs:

    import pyexcel
    import pyexcel.ext.xlsx


    pyexcel.save_as(file_name="example.xlsx", dest_file_name="exmaple.csv")

and the xlsx file is UTF-8, the dest_file is not. Even if I add `encoding="UTF-8"` nothing changes.

The only workaround I found is to use the CSV standard library:

    import csv
    import pyexcel
    import pyexcel.ext.xlsx


    sheet = pe.get_sheet(file_name="example.xlsx")
    with open("example.csv", "w", newline="", encoding="UTF-8") as csvfile:
        writer = csv.writer(csvfile)
        for row in sheet:
            writer.writerow(row)
        csvfile.close()

issue_title: csvsql: dateutils.parse gives false positive for certain strings
issue_body:
I've run into a problem where `csvsql` mistakenly casts certain fields as TIME. If a column contains only "A", "M", and "AM", `csvsql` will consider it a TIME column.

I think I've identified the use of `dateutils.parse` in [typeinference.py](https://github.com/onyxfish/csvkit/blob/master/csvkit/typeinference.py#L141) as the root of the problem. 

This is an issue with dateutil, and has been fixed as of v2.4.2. So the best fix will be to update the requirements.

If updating to dateutils@2.4.2 isn't possible, my suggested fix is to add a check if the parsed datetime exactly matches the given default. This will have the side effect of making it impossible to properly recognize a valid data field containing 9999-12-31 00:00:00.

issue_title: csvjoin includes columns on which it joins twice
Improve no_proxy support
issue_body:
Both the py2 and new py3 support for the `no_proxy` env var use a simple `endswith` check in `bypass_host`.

- https://github.com/httplib2/httplib2/blob/master/python2/httplib2/__init__.py#L843
- https://github.com/httplib2/httplib2/blob/master/python3/httplib2/__init__.py#L800

There are no standards about the behaviour of no_proxy env var, but `endswith` is probably not what you want here.

If domain starts with a dot (example: `.example.com`): 
- Use `endswith` to match any subdomain (`foo.example.com` should match)
- Remove the dot and do an exact match (`example.com` should also match)

If domain does not start with a dot (example: `example.com`): 
- It should be an exact match.

My understanding is that the current implementation of the check will match wrong cases:

```
no_proxy='bar.com'
bypass_host('foobar.com') # TRUE! 
```
Might be a good idea to add a test for it.

See this thread: curl/curl#1208

Even the curl people are struggling with understanding / documenting the correct behaviour. There does not seem to be an authoritative document about it.

issue_title: Inbound and tracking endpoints for Mandrill need two separate webhook keys
issue_body:
Hello!

I'm working on switching our use of djrill over to anymail with Django 1.8 on python 2.7 (we're working on upgrading to 1.11 and djrill is one of the final packages to go). We handle a lot of inbound messages so, I'm very excited to see this feature coming along :-)

Our current Mandrill setup has a single webhook which handles both tracking and inbound with a single key. As far as I can tell there is no way to have the same key between two different webhooks.

The anymail setup for the feature_inbound branch adds a second URL specifically for inbound and either tracking or inbound views will raise the following exception:

```
AnymailConfigurationError: You seem to have set Mandrill's *inbound* webhook URL to Anymail's Mandrill *tracking* webhook URL.
```

So the problem is:
* If we create a second webhook we would have a second key generated resulting in a signature error on one of the endpoints.
* If we use a single endpoint we raise the configuration error.

I'm considering adding a third signal and set of endpoints that handle any event type. I'm happy to submit a pull request for that but before I do it I want to check: is there already another solution in mind?

Thanks!
Jonathan

issue_title: class member variables returned by value
issue_body:
If there is a following class
```
class A {
 public:
    std::shared_ptr<B> b;
    std::string str;
};
```
and I'm exporting its member variables via `def_readonly`.
I retrieve the objects in python    
```
 import ext
 a = ext.A()
 b1 = a.b
 b2 = a.b
 s1 = a.str
 s2 = a.str
```
Then, `id(b1) == id(b2)` which is expected as `reference_internal` policy is used for `def_readonly`. 
However, `id(s1) != id(s2)` even though it has also been declared via `def_readonly`. 
Is it because `std::string` is almost a POD type and thus, getter returns a copy ?

issue_title: Difference in behavior with inheritance across modules between Windows and MacOS/Linux
issue_body:
I've encountered another issue while porting Audio ToolKit to pybind11.
There are several modules defined in the library, all driving from a parent class in a module named Core.
On Windows, I defined in the Core bindings the Python declaration fo the base class as such: https://github.com/mbrucher/AudioTK/blob/feature/pybind11/Python/Core/Core.cpp
After, in the EQ module, I declared the inheritance: https://github.com/mbrucher/AudioTK/blob/feature/pybind11/Python/EQ/EQ.cpp and when running tests, Python/pybind11 could find all the links and everything was fine.
On macOS, I got this: ImportError: generic_type: type "FloatSecondOrderBaseCoefficients" referenced unknown base type "ATK::TypedBaseFilter<float, float>"
"ATK::TypedBaseFilter<float, float>" is a type defined in the Core, and strangely, pybind11 couldn't find the definition in its memory, even if I force the import of Core before.

How is inheritance across modules supposed to work? Am I doing something crazy that no one did before?

issue_title: Optional caster r.v.p. problems
use != instead of == in make_iterator
issue_body:
a minor thing: using `!=` in make_iterator() would be compatible with C++11 range-based for.

I just noticed the difference when I tried to wrap a minimal C++ iterator that had `!=` to work with C++ for, but not `==`.

issue_title: Definition of custom type casters should be present in all compilation units
issue_body:
Let me suggest to add a warning note to the "Custom type casters" section of the documentation which informs about the following:

Custom type casters (i.e. partial overloads to the ``pybind11::detail::type_caster<T>`` template) must be defined in every compilation unit of a Python binding code, otherwise things can go wrong.

For example, I ran into a very hard to debug problem where the ``load()`` function of my custom ``type_caster`` overload was called by pybind11 but on an instance of the generic ``type_caster`` template. Since the generic template doesn't have the ``value`` field, I got a segmentation fault when my custom ``load()`` implementation tried to store the results in that class field.

Obviously, the construction of the type caster and the call to its ``load()`` method happened in different compilation units. My fault was that I didn't include the partial template overload at the top of each and every cpp file.

Perhaps it makes sense to warn other users and include a corresponding statement in the documentation (similar to the advice given on the use of the PYBIND11_MAKE_OPAQUE macro).
